Use this to group programs into broad learning types such as technical, behavioral, leadership, compliance, digital, functional, or certification-based offerings.
Example: Leadership Development
📝Delivery_Mode
InputText InputText
Primary mode through which the program is delivered.
Indicate how the learning is provided so the organization can track delivery mix, scheduling needs, and learner access patterns.
Example: Virtual Instructor-Led
🔑Vendor_IDRequired
PrimaryAuto GeneratedId
Unique identifier of the internal academy or external provider delivering the program.
Use the vendor or provider ID to connect the training program with the approved vendor master. This should support both internal and external delivery models.
Example: VEND-004
🔢Duration_Hours
InputNumeric InputNumber
Total planned learning duration of the program in hours.
Capture the expected instructional or guided-learning time in hours for analysis, learner planning, and training-load calculation.
Example: 16
🔢Duration_Days
InputNumeric InputNumber
Total planned span of the program in days.
Use this when the learning intervention runs over one or more calendar days. This helps with scheduling, release planning, and participation logistics.
Example: 2
📝Certification_Awarded
InputText InputText
Indicates whether the program grants an internal or external certificate on successful completion.
Use this field to distinguish programs that issue a completion certificate, badge, or recognized credential from those that do not.
Example: Yes
✅Mandatory_FlagRequired
InputToggleBoolean
Indicates whether the program is mandatory for a role, group, policy, or compliance need.
Mark this as yes when completion is compulsory due to regulation, policy, onboarding, role readiness, or risk mitigation requirements.
Example: Yes
📝External_Certification
InputText InputText
Name of the external credential associated with the program, if applicable.
Use this field when the program prepares learners for a third-party certification or awards a credential recognized outside the organization.
Example: Certified ScrumMaster
📝Target_Audience
InputText InputText
Primary learner population for whom the program is designed.
Describe the intended participant group so nominations and learning recommendations can be aligned to workforce segments.
Example: First-line managers
📝Target_Departments
InputText InputText
Departments or functions for which the program is primarily relevant.
Use this field when the program is designed mainly for selected functions or operational groups. Multiple departments can be listed if needed.
Example: Finance; Operations; Analytics
📝Target_Job_Levels
InputDropdownText
Role levels, career bands, or leadership levels for which the program is intended.
Use standard level codes or labels so learning can be mapped to role architecture, succession planning, and leadership pipelines.
Example: IC3; IC4; M1
📝Recertification_FrequencyRequired
InputText InputText
Required interval for refresher, recertification, or renewal of the learning program.
Capture the renewal cycle when a program or credential must be repeated to remain valid or compliant.
Example: Every 24 months
📝Criticality
InputDropdownText
Relative business importance of the program.
Use this to indicate whether the program is low, medium, high, or critical in terms of business continuity, regulatory coverage, strategic capability, or role readiness.
Example: High
Dropdown: Low, Medium, High, Critical
💰Cost_per_Participant_USD
InputNumeric InputCurrency
Estimated or standard cost per participant for the program in US dollars.
Use the average learner-level cost to support budgeting, program comparison, and training ROI analysis.
Example: 450
📆Year_Introduced
InputYear PickerYear
Year in which the program was first introduced into the learning portfolio.
Capture the first year the program became available in the organization to support portfolio maturity and lifecycle analysis.
Example: 2023
📅Last_Updated_Year
InputDate PickerDate
Most recent year in which the program design, content, or delivery model was updated.
Use this to monitor content freshness and identify programs that may require review or redesign.
Example: 2025
📝Program_Lifecycle_Status
Renamed from Status
InputDropdownText
Current lifecycle status of the training program.
Use standardized values such as Active, Planned, On Hold, Retired, or Archived so the learning catalog remains governable.
Example: Active
Dropdown: Active, Inactive, Planned, Retired
🔑Vendor_IDRequired
DependentAuto GeneratedId
Unique identifier for the vendor or learning provider.
Use a stable ID for each internal or external provider so the organization can track contracts, quality, coverage, costs, and program linkages reliably.
Example: VEND-004
📝Vendor_Name
InputText InputText
Official name of the vendor, provider, academy, or partner organization.
Capture the formal provider name used in contracting, evaluation, governance, and program records.
Example: Global Learning Partners
📝Vendor_Type
InputText InputText
Type of provider delivering the learning solution.
Use this field to classify whether the provider is internal, external, strategic, accredited, technology-based, university-based, or specialist.
Example: External Training Provider
🔢Country
InputNumeric InputNumber
Primary country of registration or operation of the vendor.
Use the vendor’s main country to support legal, procurement, and geographic coverage analysis.
Example: India
📝Coverage
InputText InputText
Geographic or business coverage supported by the vendor.
Describe whether the provider serves local, regional, national, multi-country, or global learning needs.
Example: Global
📝Specialisation
InputText InputText
Primary area of specialization of the vendor or provider.
Use this to indicate the domains in which the provider has recognized capability or service strength.
Example: Leadership and Digital Skills
📝Website
DependentText InputText
Official website or public reference link for the vendor.
Store the main website so procurement, L&D, and auditors can quickly verify provider details or offerings.
Example: www.globallearningpartners.com
📝Contact_Name
InputText InputText
Primary relationship owner or contact person at the vendor organization.
Capture the main contact used for coordination, scheduling, commercials, and escalations.
Example: Meera Rao
📝Contact_Email
InputText InputText
Official work email address of the primary vendor contact.
Use a monitored business email so communications and vendor follow-ups are routed correctly.
Example: meera.rao@globallearningpartners.com
📝Contact_Phone
InputText InputText
Phone number of the primary vendor contact.
Store a reachable business number for operational coordination, issue resolution, and urgent changes.
Example: +91 98765 43210
📝Accreditation_StatusRequired
InputDropdownText
Indicates whether the vendor holds required approvals, accreditations, or recognition relevant to the learning service.
Use this to track provider credibility, certification rights, or approved-partner status where applicable.
Example: Accredited
✅Active_Status
InputDropdownBoolean
Indicates whether the vendor is currently active for engagement.
Use standard values such as Active, Inactive, Suspended, or Under Review so provider usage is governed properly.
Example: Active
Dropdown: Active, Inactive, Planned, Retired
📈Quality_Rating
InputNumeric InputScore
Internal quality rating assigned to the vendor based on evaluation or review.
Use a standardized numeric or banded rating to support vendor comparison and selection decisions.
Example: 4.5
📈Satisfaction_Score
InputNumeric InputScore
Average learner or stakeholder satisfaction score for the vendor.
Use post-program feedback or periodic review results to track participant experience with the provider.
Example: 4.3
💰Price_Range_USD
InputNumeric InputCurrency
Typical commercial price range for engagements with the vendor in US dollars.
Capture the standard pricing band to support budgeting, sourcing decisions, and cost benchmarking.
Example: 300-700 per learner
📆Year_Engaged
InputYear PickerYear
Year in which the organization first engaged the vendor.
Use this for relationship-age analysis, renewal planning, and vendor governance history.
Example: 2022
📄Notes
InputTextareaLongtext
Additional remarks, observations, or contractual context about the vendor.
Use this field for non-structured operational notes that may help procurement, governance, or L&D teams.
Example: Strong facilitator bench for APAC virtual cohorts
📝Preferred_Vendor
InputText InputText
Indicates whether the vendor is designated as a preferred provider.
Mark yes when the provider is approved as a preferred option under sourcing or governance rules.
Example: Yes
📝Contract_Reference
DependentText InputText
Internal contract, agreement, or procurement reference number linked to the vendor.
Use a traceable contract or procurement reference so vendor records connect cleanly to legal and commercial documents.
Example: CTR-2025-118
📝Last_Reviewed
InputText InputText
Most recent date on which the vendor record or performance was formally reviewed.
Use this to govern periodic vendor evaluation, quality review, and renewal planning.
Example: 2026-01-15
🔑Record_IDRequired
PrimaryAuto GeneratedId
Unique identifier for the employee training participation record.
Use a stable record ID for each training attendance or completion instance so it can be tracked, audited, and analyzed over time.
Example: TR-2026-1048
🔑Employee_IDRequired
PrimaryAuto GeneratedId
Unique identifier of the employee associated with the training record.
Use the employee ID to connect learning history with employee master, performance, skills, compliance, and succession data.
Example: EMP-0189
📝Employee_Name
DependentText InputText
Name of the employee who attended or was assigned the program.
Store the employee name for easy review, while the employee ID remains the primary reference key.
Example: Rohan Verma
📝Role
InputText InputText
Job role of the employee at the time of training.
Capture the role held by the employee when the program was assigned or completed. This helps training analysis by role segment.
Example: Data Analyst
📝Department
PrimaryText InputText
Department or function of the employee at the time of the training record.
Use this to analyze participation, completion, and learning investment by organizational function.
Example: Analytics
📝Location
InputText InputText
Location, office, site, or operating unit associated with the employee or training participation.
Use the employee or assignment location so training coverage can be reported across geographies or operating units.
Example: Bengaluru
🔑Program_IDRequired
DependentAuto GeneratedId
Unique identifier of the training program attended or assigned.
Use the program ID to link learning records to the governed training catalog.
Example: TP-2026-014
📝Program_Name
DependentText InputText
Name of the training program linked to the record.
Store the program name for readability in reports, even though the program ID should remain the key reference.
Example: Advanced Data Analysis for Managers
📝Program_Category
InputDropdownText
Category of the training program associated with the record.
Use this for training analytics by learning type such as compliance, leadership, digital, technical, or behavioral.
Example: Leadership Development
🔑Vendor_IDRequired
PrimaryAuto GeneratedId
Identifier of the internal or external provider that delivered the program.
Use the vendor ID to connect participation records with the vendor master and provider analytics.
Example: VEND-004
📅Start_Date
InputDate PickerDate
Date on which the employee started the training program.
Capture the start date to support learning timelines, scheduling analytics, and auditability.
Example: 2026-02-10
📅End_Date
InputDate PickerDate
Date on which the employee completed or ended the training program.
Use the end date to measure program completion timing, validity periods, and learning-cycle tracking.
Example: 2026-02-11
🔢Duration_Hours
InputNumeric InputNumber
Total training duration in hours recorded for the employee instance.
Use this to calculate learner effort, aggregate learning hours, and reporting on time invested in development.
Example: 16
🔢Duration_DaysRequired
InputNumeric InputNumber
Total training duration in days recorded for the employee instance.
Use this when the intervention spans multiple days and day-based scheduling or absence analysis is required.
Example: 2
📝Delivery_Mode
InputText InputText
Actual delivery mode used for the training instance.
Capture how the employee received the training so completion and satisfaction can be analyzed by delivery model.
Example: Virtual Instructor-Led
📝Completion_Status
InputDropdownText
Current or final completion outcome of the training record.
Use standardized values such as Assigned, In Progress, Completed, Cancelled, or No Show.
Example: Completed
📈Assessment_Score
InputNumeric InputScore
Score achieved by the employee in the training assessment, if applicable.
Use this to track knowledge checks, certification exams, or program evaluations tied to the learning intervention.
Example: 86
✅Passed_Flag
InputToggleBoolean
Indicates whether the employee met the passing requirement for the assessment or program.
Mark yes when the learner successfully meets the program’s pass criteria or qualifying threshold.
Example: Yes
📝Certificate_Awarded
InputText InputText
Indicates whether a certificate or credential was awarded for the completed program.
Use this to distinguish simple attendance from formally recognized completion.
Example: Yes
📅Certificate_Expiry_DateRequired
InputDate PickerDate
Expiry date of the certificate or credential, if one applies.
Use this field when the certificate has a validity period and recertification tracking is required.
Example: 2028-02-11
💰Cost_USD
InputNumeric InputCurrency
Total cost attributed to the training record in US dollars.
Capture the learner-level cost so training spend can be analyzed by person, function, program, or vendor.
Example: 450
📆Year
InputYear PickerYear
Calendar year associated with the training record for reporting and trend analysis.
Use this as the reporting year of the training instance so participation, spend, and completion can be analyzed over time.
Example: 2026
🔑Calendar_IDRequired
PrimaryAuto GeneratedId
Unique identifier for the learning governance calendar entry.
Use a stable ID for each planned learning, review, governance, or capability event so it can be tracked across planning cycles.
Example: CAL-2026-021
📆Year
InputYear PickerYear
Calendar year in which the event is planned or scheduled.
Use the year to organize governance events and learning activities across annual planning cycles.
Example: 2026
📝Quarter
InputDropdownText
Quarter of the year in which the event falls.
Use standard values such as Q1, Q2, Q3, or Q4 for planning, reporting, and sequencing of governance activities.
Example: Q2
📝Month
InputText InputText
Month in which the event is scheduled.
Capture the month to support detailed planning and timeline visualization within the annual learning calendar.
Example: May
📝Event_Name
InputText InputText
Name of the planned learning or governance event.
Use a clear event title that helps L&D, governance teams, and business stakeholders understand the purpose of the activity.
Example: Quarterly Learning Governance Review
📝Event_Type
InputText InputText
Type of event recorded in the learning governance calendar.
Use this to distinguish between governance reviews, program launches, capability councils, audits, nomination windows, or training events.
Example: Governance Review
📝Target_Audience
InputText InputText
Primary audience or stakeholder group relevant to the event.
Capture who the event is for or who is expected to participate, such as employees, managers, L&D leads, or governance committees.
Example: Business Unit L&D Leads
🔑Program_IDRequired
DependentAuto GeneratedId
Identifier of the related training program, if the calendar entry is linked to a program.
Use this when the event refers to a specific learning intervention, launch, review, or program cycle. Leave blank if the event is purely governance-related.
Example: TP-2026-014
🔑Vendor_IDRequired
PrimaryAuto GeneratedId
Identifier of the associated vendor or provider, if relevant.
Use this field when the event involves a delivery partner, assessment partner, or provider review.
Example: VEND-004
📅Planned_Start_Date
InputDate PickerDate
Planned start date of the event.
Capture the intended start date for the activity so the calendar can support operational planning and coordination.
Example: 2026-05-12
📅Planned_End_Date
InputDate PickerDate
Planned end date of the event.
Use the end date to define the event window and support tracking of milestones or multi-day activities.
Example: 2026-05-12
📝Governance_Owner
InputText InputText
Role or person accountable for overall governance ownership of the event.
Use this field to identify who is responsible for the event from a governance or coordination standpoint.
Example: Head of Learning Governance
📝Learning_Lead
InputText InputText
Role or person managing the learning-side planning or execution of the event.
Capture the L&D owner or program lead responsible for preparation, coordination, and execution.
Example: Regional Learning Manager
📄Description
InputTextareaLongtext
Short description of the purpose or agenda of the event.
Use this to summarize what the event covers, why it exists, and how it fits into the learning governance cycle.
Example: Quarterly review of program participation, completion, vendor quality, and capability priorities
📝Audit_Lifecycle_Status
Renamed from Status
InputDropdownText
Current status of the calendar event.
Use standardized values such as Planned, Confirmed, Completed, Deferred, or Cancelled.
Example: Planned
📄Completion_Notes
InputTextareaLongtext
Notes recorded after completion or closure of the event.
Use this to capture outcomes, decisions, observations, or follow-up actions after the event occurs.
Example: Approved Q3 compliance rollout and vendor shortlist
📝Criticality
InputText InputText
Relative importance of the calendar entry to learning governance or capability delivery.
Use this to distinguish routine activities from high-impact reviews, launches, or compliance-related milestones.
Example: High
🔑Audit_IDRequired
PrimaryAuto GeneratedId
Unique identifier for each governance or compliance audit record.
Use a stable audit ID so each audit can be tracked across planning, execution, findings, corrective actions, follow-ups, and trend reporting over multiple years.
Example: AUD-2025-014
📆Year
InputYear PickerYear
Calendar year to which the audit record belongs, maintained as a historical annual series.
Capture one row per audit instance within the relevant year. This sheet should support last 5 years or more of historical audit tracking, not just a single reporting-year snapshot.
Example: 2025
📝Audit_Type
InputDropdownText
Type of audit conducted.
Classify the audit based on its purpose, such as internal audit, external audit, regulatory audit, certification audit, process audit, or governance review.
Example: Internal Audit
📝Regulatory_Body
DependentDropdownText
Authority, standard-setting body, or oversight function associated with the audit.
Use this field when the audit is linked to a regulator, certification body, industry standard, or internal oversight committee. For purely internal audits, use the relevant internal authority or governance function.
Example: Internal Audit Committee
📝Location
InputText InputText
Location, site, function, or operating unit covered by the audit.
Capture the location or organizational unit that was audited so findings and corrective actions can be analyzed by geography, business unit, or operational area.
Example: Corporate Office - Bengaluru
📝Audit_Category
InputDropdownText
Broad theme or domain covered by the audit.
Use this to group audits into categories such as compliance, finance, HR, data privacy, information security, operations, quality, ESG, or governance.
Example: Information Security
📝Quarter
InputText InputText
Quarter in which the audit was conducted or reported.
Helps track audit scheduling cadence, seasonal concentration, and governance coverage across the year.
Example: Q3
📅Start_Date
InputDate PickerDate
Date on which the audit activity formally started.
Use the planned or actual start date of the audit engagement. This supports scheduling analysis and duration tracking.
Example: 2025-07-08
📅End_Date
InputDate PickerDate
Date on which the audit activity formally ended.
Use the planned or actual closure date of the audit fieldwork or review. This supports duration and timeliness analysis.
Example: 2025-07-18
📝Audit_Method
InputText InputText
Method used to conduct the audit.
Capture how the audit was performed, such as on-site, remote, hybrid, document review, interview-based, system-based, or sampling-led review.
Example: Hybrid
📈Score
InputNumeric InputScore
Overall audit score, rating, or percentage outcome.
Use the audit scoring approach defined by the organization or standard body. This may be a percentage, maturity score, or graded rating converted into a comparable value.
Example: 88
📝OutcomeRequired
InputText InputText
Final audit result or disposition.
Summarize the headline outcome such as passed, conditionally passed, observation raised, major non-conformance, or improvement required.
Example: Passed with Observations
📝Key_Findings
InputText InputText
Summary of the most important findings, gaps, or observations from the audit.
Capture concise but meaningful findings that can later drive remediation tracking, risk prioritization, and governance reporting.
Example: Access review frequency was inconsistent across two critical systems.
📝Corrective_Actions
InputText InputText
Summary of agreed remediation or control-improvement actions.
Record the corrective actions expected from the audited entity, including control fixes, process changes, documentation updates, or training actions.
Example: Implement quarterly access reviews and update control owner sign-off process.
📝Audit_Lifecycle_Status
Renamed from Status
InputDropdownText
Current lifecycle status of the audit record.
Use a standardized status such as planned, in progress, completed, closed, remediation pending, or overdue.
Example: Closed
🔑Audit_Lead_Employee_IDRequired
DependentAuto GeneratedId
Employee ID of the internal audit lead or accountable owner.
Link the audit to the responsible employee or governance owner for accountability, escalation, and audit workload analysis.
Example: EMP-0048
📝Risk_Level
InputDropdownText
Overall risk severity associated with the audit result.
Use a standard risk scale to show the seriousness of the audit outcome or unresolved findings.
Example: Medium
🔑Log_IDRequired
DependentAuto GeneratedId
Unique identifier for each audit trail or system activity log entry.
Use a stable log ID so every tracked action in the platform can be referenced, investigated, and audited independently.
Example: LOG-2026-000184
📅Timestamp
InputDate PickerDate
Exact date and time when the logged event occurred.
Capture the event timestamp in a consistent date-time format so actions can be sequenced accurately during reviews, investigations, and compliance checks.
Example: 2026-01-14 10:42:33
🔑User_IDRequired
DependentAuto GeneratedId
Unique identifier of the user who triggered the logged action.
Link each system action to a unique user account or employee identity for accountability and access-governance monitoring.
Example: USR-00421
📝User_Role
InputText InputText
Application or organizational role of the user at the time of the action.
Use this to understand what type of actor performed the event, such as HR Admin, Business Unit Head, Manager, Auditor, Analyst, or System Admin.
Example: HR Admin
📝Module_Name
InputText InputText
System module or functional area where the action occurred.
Identify the platform area impacted by the action so audit trails can be analyzed by process domain or data object group.
Example: Employee_Master
📝Action_Type
InputDropdownText
Type of action recorded in the log.
Use standardized action labels such as create, update, delete, approve, reject, export, login, logout, or view sensitive record.
Example: Update
📝Record_Reference
DependentText InputText
Reference ID of the business record affected by the action.
Store the target record identifier so investigators can trace exactly which employee, policy, audit, training, or assessment record was touched.
Example: EMP-0048
📝Change_ReasonRequired
InputDropdownText
Reason or justification provided for the change, if applicable.
Capture the business reason for a change when required for approval-controlled or compliance-sensitive updates.
Example: Corrected employee work mode after manager approval.
📝Action_Processing_Status
Renamed from Status
InputText InputText
Processing or review status of the logged event.
Use this to show whether the action completed successfully, failed, is pending review, or was flagged for investigation.
Example: Successful
✅Sensitive_Data_Flag
InputToggleBoolean
Indicates whether the action involved sensitive, restricted, or high-risk data.
Mark this as yes when the activity touched personal data, compensation data, performance records, compliance evidence, or other controlled information.
Example: Yes
📝Risk_Level
InputDropdownText
Risk severity associated with the logged action.
Use a standard scale to highlight events that may require heightened monitoring, approval review, or security investigation.
Example: Low
📝Approved_ByRequired
InputDropdownText
User or approver who authorized the action, where approval was required.
Capture the approving authority for sensitive or workflow-controlled changes to support traceability and control evidence.
Example: USR-00012
📝IP_Address
InputText InputText
IP address or network source from which the action originated.
Useful for security monitoring, suspicious access review, and audit investigation. Use IPv4 or IPv6 format as captured by the platform.
Example: 10.18.44.27
📝System_Version
InputText InputText
Application release or system version active when the event occurred.
Helps trace issues to a specific platform version and supports change-management or incident review.
Example: v3.4.2
📆Year
CalculatedYear PickerYear
Calendar year of the log event for easier annual analysis and archival.
Store the derived year from the timestamp to support yearly aggregation, retention policies, and reporting.
Example: 2026

DEI & Culture

2 tabs · 36 fields
📆Year
InputYear PickerYear
Calendar year for the diversity, equity, and inclusion statistics record, maintained as a historical annual series.
Use one row per year so the organization can track trends over the last 5 years or more. This is a yearly analytics input, not a single one-time reporting field.
Example: 2025
🔢Total_Employees
InputNumeric InputNumber
Total active employee count included in the DEI snapshot for the year.
Use the employee population that falls within the measurement scope for the stated year. This becomes the base denominator for several percentage metrics.
Example: 1240
📊Male_%
InputNumeric InputPercentage
Percentage of employees identified as male in the covered population.
Use this when the organization tracks binary gender representation as part of its DEI reporting framework. Ensure the value is based on the defined employee population for the year.
Example: 54.0%
📊Female_%
InputNumeric InputPercentage
Percentage of employees identified as female in the covered population.
Use this when the organization tracks binary gender representation as part of its DEI reporting framework. Ensure the value is based on the defined employee population for the year.
Example: 44.0%
📊Other_or_Undeclared_Gender_%
InputNumeric InputPercentage
Percentage of employees identified as another gender category or who have not declared gender.
Use this field to capture broader gender inclusion reporting where applicable and permitted by law or internal policy.
Example: 2.0%
📊Demographic_Group_A_%
InputNumeric InputPercentage
Percentage representation of the first tracked demographic group under the organization’s approved diversity framework.
Use a standardized internal definition for this group so comparisons remain consistent over time. Replace the label in implementation if the organization uses named demographic categories.
Example: 18.0%
📊Demographic_Group_B_%
InputNumeric InputPercentage
Percentage representation of the second tracked demographic group under the organization’s approved diversity framework.
Use a consistent internal classification model and only populate where lawful and relevant.
Example: 42.0%
📊Demographic_Group_C_%
InputNumeric InputPercentage
Percentage representation of the third tracked demographic group under the organization’s approved diversity framework.
This keeps the model generic across countries and legal contexts while still supporting demographic analysis.
Example: 27.0%
📊Non_Management_%
InputNumeric InputPercentage
Percentage of employees in non-management roles.
Use this to understand how representation is distributed in the broader workforce outside formal management roles.
Example: 78.0%
📊Middle_Management_%
InputNumeric InputPercentage
Percentage of employees in middle-management roles.
Use this to analyze representation within people-management and business leadership tiers.
Example: 16.0%
📊Senior_Leadership_%
InputNumeric InputPercentage
Percentage of employees in senior-leadership roles.
Use this to assess representation at the top of the organization and monitor leadership inclusion over time.
Example: 6.0%
📊Under_35_%
InputNumeric InputPercentage
Percentage of employees below age 35 in the measured population.
Use this as part of age-diversity analysis and workforce-shape monitoring.
Example: 31.0%
📊Age_35_to_50_%
InputNumeric InputPercentage
Percentage of employees between ages 35 and 50 in the measured population.
Use consistent age-band definitions across years to preserve comparability.
Example: 49.0%
📊Over_50_%
InputNumeric InputPercentage
Percentage of employees above age 50 in the measured population.
Supports age-diversity analysis, retirement-risk interpretation, and inclusion planning.
Example: 20.0%
📊Disability_Inclusion_%
InputNumeric InputPercentage
Percentage of employees who are identified within the organization’s disability inclusion reporting scope.
Use only where legally permitted and supported by the organization’s disclosure and privacy framework.
Example: 3.5%
📊Gender_Pay_Gap_%
InputNumeric InputPercentage
Percentage pay gap observed between gender groups based on the organization’s defined DEI methodology.
Use a clearly documented calculation approach so the number is consistent and auditable across years.
Example: 6.2%
📊Demographic_Pay_Gap_%
InputNumeric InputPercentage
Percentage pay gap observed between tracked demographic groups based on the organization’s approved methodology.
Keep the metric definition stable and compliant with local legal requirements.
Example: 4.8%
📈DEI_Index_Score
InputNumeric InputScore
Composite index score summarizing overall DEI health for the year.
Use this as a summarized indicator built from representation, equity, inclusion, policy, and participation metrics under a defined scoring model.
Example: 74
📊DEI_Training_Completion_%
InputNumeric InputPercentage
Percentage of in-scope employees who completed assigned DEI-related training in the year.
Useful for compliance, culture, and awareness reporting across the organization.
Example: 91.0%
📄DEI_Narrative
InputTextareaLongtext
Short narrative describing key DEI progress, challenges, or notable changes during the year.
Add concise context behind the numbers so stakeholders can understand what improved, where gaps remain, and what actions were taken.
Example: Improved gender balance in mid-level roles but leadership representation remained flat.
📈Progress_Rating
InputNumeric InputScore
Overall rating of DEI progress for the year.
Use a consistent rating scale such as improving, stable, needs attention, or declining to simplify executive interpretation.
Example: Improving
📆Year
InputYear PickerYear
Calendar year for the culture indicator record, maintained as a historical annual series.
Use one row per year to support multi-year culture trend analysis. This should represent a yearly view over the last 5 years or more, not a one-time reporting field.
Example: 2025
🔢Employee_Count
InputNumeric InputNumber
Total employee count included in the culture measurement scope for the year.
Use the defined population covered by engagement surveys, culture assessments, and other culture signals for the relevant year.
Example: 1185
📈eNPS_Score
CalculatedNumeric InputScore
Employee Net Promoter Score for the year.
Capture the standard eNPS value derived from promoter and detractor responses to measure employee advocacy.
Example: 32
📊eNPS_Promoters_%
InputNumeric InputPercentage
Percentage of promoter respondents in the eNPS calculation.
Use the share of respondents classified as promoters under the organization’s eNPS method.
Example: 46.0%
📈Manager_Effectiveness_Score
InputNumeric InputScore
Average score reflecting employee perception of manager effectiveness.
Use survey or assessment-based inputs that evaluate coaching quality, feedback, support, and people-management capability.
Example: 4.1/5
📈Psychological_Safety_Index
InputNumeric InputScore
Composite score reflecting how safe employees feel to speak up, challenge ideas, or admit mistakes.
Use a defined internal or survey-based methodology to quantify psychological safety across the organization.
Example: 78
📈Operational_Compliance_Culture_Score
InputNumeric InputScore
Score reflecting how strongly the organization’s culture supports disciplined adherence to critical operational, quality, safety, or compliance practices.
Use this as a generic operational-discipline culture metric rather than an industry-specific measure. It can represent safety culture, quality culture, process discipline, or control culture depending on the business context.
Example: 81
📈Collaboration_Index
InputNumeric InputScore
Composite score reflecting cross-team collaboration and knowledge sharing.
Use inputs such as survey responses, network analysis, or leadership assessments to understand how well teams work across boundaries.
Example: 76
📈Learning_Culture_Score
InputNumeric InputScore
Score indicating how strongly the culture supports learning, growth, and skill development.
Use signals such as manager support for learning, employee participation, experimentation, and knowledge-sharing behavior.
Example: 83
📈Trust_in_Leadership_Score
InputNumeric InputScore
Score reflecting employee trust in senior leadership.
Use survey-based or composite signals that measure confidence in leadership decisions, transparency, and organizational direction.
Example: 74
📈Work_Life_Balance_Score
InputNumeric InputScore
Score reflecting employee perception of workload sustainability and work-life balance.
Use survey responses or workforce analytics that capture balance, flexibility, and burnout-related concerns.
Example: 3.8/5
📊Voluntary_Attrition_%
InputNumeric InputPercentage
Percentage of employees who exited voluntarily during the year.
Useful as a culture-adjacent outcome signal when read alongside engagement, trust, and retention indicators.
Example: 11.5%
📊Engagement_Survey_Participation_%
InputNumeric InputPercentage
Percentage of in-scope employees who participated in the culture or engagement survey.
High participation strengthens confidence in the representativeness of culture metrics.
Example: 87.0%
📄Culture_Narrative
InputTextareaLongtext
Short narrative describing the year’s cultural strengths, concerns, and notable shifts.
Use this field to explain the context behind movement in culture metrics and to document major interventions or risks.
Example: Manager effectiveness improved after coaching interventions, but work-life balance remains a concern in delivery teams.
📈External_Employer_Rating
InputNumeric InputScore
External employer-review or reputation score used as a supporting culture signal.
Use a normalized rating from an external review platform or employer-brand source where available. Keep the source consistent over time for comparability.
Example: 4.0/5

ESG & Sustainability

3 tabs · 49 fields
📆Year
InputYear PickerYear
Calendar year for the recorded environmental-impact metrics, maintained as a historical annual series.
Capture one row per year so the organization can track environmental performance trends across the last 5 years or more, not just a single reporting snapshot.
Example: 2025
🔢Employee_Count
InputNumeric InputNumber
Total number of employees for the relevant year, used for normalization of impact metrics.
Use the year-end or annual average employee count consistently so per-employee metrics remain comparable across years.
Example: 2450
📝Total_Carbon_Emissions_tCO2e
InputText InputText
Total greenhouse-gas emissions for the year measured in tonnes of carbon dioxide equivalent.
This should represent the full annual emissions footprint consolidated across all included operations, sites, and business activities for the reporting boundary.
Example: 18450
📝Scope1_tCO2e
InputDropdownText
Direct greenhouse-gas emissions from owned or controlled sources, measured in tonnes of carbon dioxide equivalent.
Include direct fuel combustion, company-owned vehicle emissions, onsite generators, and similar sources that fall within Scope 1 accounting.
Example: 3250
📝Scope2_tCO2e
DependentText InputText
Indirect greenhouse-gas emissions from purchased electricity, heating, cooling, or steam, measured in tonnes of carbon dioxide equivalent.
Capture energy-related emissions linked to purchased utilities consumed by the organization within the reporting year.
Example: 4620
📝Scope3_tCO2e
InputText InputText
Other indirect greenhouse-gas emissions across the value chain, measured in tonnes of carbon dioxide equivalent.
Use this for travel, logistics, purchased goods and services, waste, commuting, downstream use, and other material Scope 3 categories as applicable.
Example: 10580
📊Renewable_Energy_%
InputNumeric InputPercentage
Percentage of total energy consumption sourced from renewable energy.
Record the share of total consumed energy that comes from renewable procurement, onsite renewable generation, or certified green-energy arrangements.
Example: 38.5
📝Onsite_Renewable_Generation_kWh_000s
InputText InputText
Electricity generated from onsite renewable sources during the year, expressed in thousands of kilowatt-hours.
Use this field to capture output from onsite renewable assets such as rooftop solar, wind, biogas, or other local clean-energy systems.
Example: 1260
📝Total_Water_Usage_m3
InputText InputText
Total water consumed during the year, measured in cubic meters.
Capture total freshwater and process-use water drawn or consumed across all in-scope locations for the reporting boundary.
Example: 182000
📝Operational_Water_Usage_m3
InputText InputText
Water used in core operations, service delivery, production, or processing activities, measured in cubic meters.
Use this field to isolate water consumed directly by business operations rather than office, sanitation, or facility-support uses.
Example: 124500
📝Facility_Water_Usage_m3
InputText InputText
Water used for facility-support purposes such as sanitation, cleaning, landscaping, and cooling infrastructure, measured in cubic meters.
This helps separate operational water intensity from general building and infrastructure usage.
Example: 57500
📊Water_Recycled_%
InputNumeric InputPercentage
Percentage of total water usage that is recycled, reused, or recovered.
Record the proportion of annual water demand met through internal reuse, recycling systems, or recovery mechanisms.
Example: 21.0
📝Waste_Generated_tonnes_per_employee
InputText InputText
Total waste generated per employee during the year, measured in tonnes per employee.
Normalize total waste by employee count so year-over-year and cross-entity comparisons are meaningful.
Example: 0.84
📊Waste_to_Landfill_%
InputNumeric InputPercentage
Percentage of generated waste sent to landfill.
Track how much waste is not diverted through reuse, recycling, composting, recovery, or responsible disposal alternatives.
Example: 14.2
📝Priority_Material_Reduction_Tonnes
InputText InputText
Annual reduction achieved in a priority material or packaging category, measured in tonnes.
Use this field for the material-reduction metric most relevant to the organization, such as plastic, paper, metal, or another high-impact input.
Example: 42
📈ESG_Reporting_Score
InputNumeric InputScore
Internal or external score reflecting the quality, completeness, or maturity of ESG disclosure and reporting.
This can be sourced from a scoring framework, assurance review, disclosure benchmark, or internal governance assessment.
Example: 78
📝Sustainability_Maturity_Level
InputDropdownText
Overall maturity stage of the organization’s sustainability management approach.
Use a standardized maturity scale such as Initial, Developing, Defined, Managed, or Optimized to indicate how advanced the sustainability program is.
Example: Managed
📝Reporting_Framework
InputDropdownText
Primary sustainability or ESG reporting framework used for disclosure.
Capture the framework or standard applied during reporting, such as GRI, SASB, TCFD, ESRS, BRSR, or an internal reporting model.
Example: GRI
📝Key_Initiatives
InputText InputText
Summary of major environmental or sustainability initiatives active during the year.
Use a concise summary of the highest-impact actions undertaken during the year, such as renewable-energy expansion, waste reduction, water recovery, or supplier-engagement programs.
Example: Expanded rooftop solar, launched waste-segregation program, and scaled water-reuse infrastructure
📝ESG_Program_Status
InputDropdownText
Current overall status of the environmental or ESG program for the year.
Use a simple status value that reflects whether the environmental program is on track, delayed, under review, or completed for the year.
Example: On Track
🔑Initiative_IDRequired
PrimaryAuto GeneratedId
Unique identifier for each environmental, resource-efficiency, or sustainability initiative.
Use a stable initiative ID so each project can be tracked across planning, budgeting, implementation, reporting, and impact measurement.
Example: GI-2026-014
📝Initiative_Name
InputText InputText
Name of the green or sustainability initiative.
Use a clear program name that identifies the initiative and makes it easy to distinguish from other environmental improvement efforts.
Example: Workplace Energy Efficiency Upgrade
📝Initiative_Type
InputDropdownText
Type or category of the initiative.
Classify the initiative into categories such as energy efficiency, renewable energy, waste reduction, water stewardship, emissions reduction, circularity, sustainable procurement, or awareness campaign.
Example: Energy Efficiency
📝Location
InputText InputText
Location, site, function, or business unit where the initiative is being implemented.
Capture the primary place or organizational unit associated with the initiative so impact can be analyzed by geography or operating area.
Example: Corporate Office - Bengaluru
📆Start_Year
InputYear PickerYear
Year in which the initiative formally began.
Use the year the project was approved, launched, or operationally initiated.
Example: 2024
📆End_Year
InputYear PickerYear
Planned or actual year in which the initiative ends.
Leave blank for ongoing initiatives or use the target completion year if the program is still in progress.
Example: 2026
📝Initiative_Status
Renamed from Status
InputDropdownText
Current status of the initiative.
Use a standardized lifecycle status such as Planned, In Progress, On Hold, Completed, or Closed.
Example: In Progress
💰Budget_USD
InputNumeric InputCurrency
Approved budget allocated to the initiative in US dollars.
Capture the planned funding baseline for the initiative so spend and impact can be tracked against expectations.
Example: 250000
💰Actual_Spend_USD
InputNumeric InputCurrency
Actual amount spent on the initiative in US dollars.
Use cumulative actual spend to monitor execution discipline, budget variance, and program efficiency.
Example: 167500
🔑Initiative_Lead_Employee_IDRequired
DependentAuto GeneratedId
Unique identifier of the employee leading the initiative.
Link the initiative to an accountable owner so governance, approvals, and performance tracking can be assigned clearly.
Example: EMP-0148
📄Description
InputTextareaLongtext
Short description of the initiative and what it aims to improve.
Summarize the scope, objective, and intervention approach in plain business language.
Example: Upgrade lighting, HVAC controls, and power-management settings to reduce electricity use across offices
📝Impact_Achieved
InputText InputText
Summary of measurable or observed impact achieved so far.
Use this field to capture quantitative or qualitative results such as energy savings, emissions reduction, waste diverted, or behavior-change outcomes.
Example: Reduced annual electricity consumption by 11% in pilot locations
📝Strategic_Priority
InputDropdownText
Priority level assigned to the initiative from a strategic planning perspective.
Use a standard prioritization scale such as High, Medium, or Low based on business relevance, environmental impact, and executive focus.
Example: High
📝Funding_Source
InputText InputText
Primary source of funding for the initiative.
Identify whether the initiative is funded through operating budget, capex, CSR/ESG budget, grant, donor support, or another source.
Example: Capex Budget
📝Reported_in_ESG_Report
InputToggleText
Indicator showing whether the initiative is disclosed in the organization’s ESG or sustainability report.
Use Yes/No or equivalent values so reporting coverage can be monitored consistently.
Example: Yes
Dropdown: Yes, No
🔑Program_IDRequired
PrimaryAuto GeneratedId
Unique identifier for each corporate social responsibility or community-impact program.
Use a stable program ID so each CSR initiative can be tracked across approvals, budgets, beneficiaries, reporting cycles, and impact reviews.
Example: CSR-2026-008
📝Program_Name
InputText InputText
Name of the CSR or community-impact program.
Use a clear title that distinguishes the program from other community, philanthropy, volunteering, or social-impact initiatives.
Example: Digital Skills for Youth Program
📝Program_Type
InputDropdownText
Type or category of the CSR program.
Classify the program into categories such as education, healthcare, livelihood, inclusion, environment, community infrastructure, employee volunteering, or philanthropy.
Example: Education
📝Primary_Location
InputText InputText
Primary location, community, region, or operating area where the program is delivered.
Capture the main geography or beneficiary area associated with the program.
Example: Pune, Maharashtra
📆Launch_Year
InputYear PickerYear
Year in which the CSR program was launched.
Use the year when the program formally started or was first implemented.
Example: 2023
📝Program_Status
Renamed from Status
InputDropdownText
Current status of the CSR program.
Use a standard program status such as Planned, Active, On Hold, Completed, or Closed.
Example: Active
💰Annual_Budget_USD
InputNumeric InputCurrency
Annual budget allocated to the CSR program in US dollars.
Capture the yearly approved budget for the program so social-investment planning and utilization can be analyzed.
Example: 120000
🔢Beneficiary_Count_Annual
InputNumeric InputNumber
Number of beneficiaries reached by the program during the year.
Record the annual count of people, communities, institutions, or groups directly served by the program.
Example: 1850
📄Description
InputTextareaLongtext
Short description of the program and its intended social objective.
Summarize what the program does, who it serves, and what outcome it seeks to create.
Example: Provides foundational digital-skills training and career-readiness workshops for underserved youth
📄Impact_Summary
InputTextareaLongtext
Summary of measurable or observed program impact.
Capture headline results such as completion numbers, placement outcomes, health improvements, participation growth, or community reach.
Example: 1,850 learners reached and 420 participants completed the full program track
🔑Lead_Employee_IDRequired
DependentAuto GeneratedId
Unique identifier of the employee or program owner responsible for coordination.
Link the program to an accountable internal owner for governance, reporting, and stakeholder management.
Example: EMP-0097
📝Strategic_Category
InputDropdownText
Broader strategic theme to which the CSR program contributes.
Use a standard theme such as community development, education access, employability, inclusion, environmental stewardship, or social well-being.
Example: Employability
📝Priority
InputDropdownText
Priority level assigned to the CSR program.
Use a standard prioritization scale such as High, Medium, or Low based on strategic relevance, social need, and governance focus.
Example: High
📝Reported_in_ESG_Report
InputToggleText
Indicator showing whether the program is disclosed in the organization’s ESG or sustainability report.
Use Yes/No or equivalent values so external reporting coverage can be tracked consistently.
Example: Yes
Dropdown: Yes, No

Assessments

4 tabs · 105 fields
🔑Assessment_Record_IDRequired
PrimaryAuto GeneratedId
Unique identifier for each skill-assessment evidence record.
Use a stable record ID for every source-level assessment event so repeated evidence for the same employee and skill can be tracked separately across cycles, assessors, and methods.
Example: ESA-2026-000145
🔑Employee_IDRequired
DependentAuto GeneratedId
Unique identifier of the employee being assessed.
Links the record to Employee_Master and allows multiple evidence rows for the same employee-skill pair without ambiguity.
Example: EMP-01024
📝Employee_Name
DependentText InputText
Readable name of the assessed employee.
Included for business readability and validation; should match the employee master for reporting and audit checks.
Example: Aarav Mehta
🔑Skill_IDRequired
PrimaryAuto GeneratedId
Unique identifier of the skill being assessed.
Use the official skill ID from the Skills master so all evidence rolls up to the correct capability object.
Example: SKL-0421
📝Skill_Name
PrimaryText InputText
Readable name of the assessed skill.
Display label for review and reporting; should align to the master label tied to Skill_ID.
Example: Data Analysis
📝Assessment_Source_Type
InputText InputText
Broad source category from which the evidence was obtained.
Examples include Self Assessment, Manager Assessment, Observation, Focus Group Discussion, Test, Certification, Project Evidence, Peer Feedback, or Trainer Evaluation.
Example: Observation
📝Assessment_Source_Subtype
InputText InputText
More specific sub-classification of the source.
Use this when the broad source type needs further detail, such as Structured Observation, Practical Demo, Panel Interview, or LMS Final Test.
Example: Structured Observation
📝Assessment_Method
InputMulti SelectText
Method or instrument used to capture the evidence.
Helps describe how the evidence was produced, such as rating scale, objective test, checklist, simulation, interview guide, or rubric-based observation.
Example: 5-point rubric
📝Assessment_Cycle
InputText InputText
Assessment period or cycle in which the skill evidence was captured.
Use this field to group repeated skill assessments into formal cycles such as annual reviews, quarterly reviews, certification windows, or program-based evaluation rounds.
Example: FY2025-26 Annual Capability Review
📅Assessment_Date
InputDate PickerDate
Date on which the evidence was recorded or validated.
Use the actual date of the assessment event, not just the reporting year, so repeated evidence can be ordered correctly.
Example: 2026-03-10
📆Assessment_Year
InputYear PickerYear
Calendar or fiscal year to which the assessment instance belongs.
Used for quick year-based filtering, reporting, and trend analysis without deriving the year repeatedly from Assessment_Date. This should align to the organization’s reporting convention, such as calendar year or fiscal year.
Example: 2026
📝Assessment_Quarter
InputText InputText
Quarter in which the assessment was conducted.
Captures the reporting quarter for the assessment instance so recurring assessments can be grouped into Q1, Q2, Q3, or Q4 for trend, cycle, and dashboard analysis.
Example: Q1
🔑Assessor_IDRequired
PrimaryAuto GeneratedId
Identifier of the person, panel, or system that produced the assessment.
Supports traceability where a manager, assessor, trainer, panel, or platform generated the rating.
Example: MGR-0087
📝Assessor_Name
InputText InputText
Readable name of the assessor, panel, or system owner.
Human-readable counterpart to Assessor_ID for review and governance.
Example: Nisha Rao
📈Raw_Score
InputNumeric InputScore
Original score or rating captured from the source.
Store the score exactly as produced by that source before normalization, weighting, or cross-source consolidation.
Example: 4
📈Normalized_Score
InputNumeric InputScore
Standardized score converted to the common comparison scale.
Use this when different sources use different scales so all evidence can later be triangulated consistently.
Example: 80
📈Score_Scale
InputNumeric InputScore
Scale on which the source score should be interpreted.
Clarifies whether the score came from a 1-5 rating, 0-100 test score, pass/fail result, decile, or another scale.
Example: 1-5
📝Proficiency_Level
InputDropdownText
Mapped proficiency level inferred from this evidence source alone.
Use the standard capability scale for source-level interpretation only; the final validated level should be finalized in Data_Triangulation.
Example: Level 3 — Proficient
📈Confidence_Score
InputNumeric InputScore
Confidence or reliability score assigned to the evidence source.
Useful when some sources are more reliable than others; this can later influence triangulation weights.
Example: 0.85
📝Validation_Status
InputDropdownText
Status showing whether the evidence has been reviewed and accepted for use.
Prevents draft, incomplete, stale, or rejected evidence from flowing into downstream capability logic.
Example: Validated
📄Evidence_Notes
InputTextareaLongtext
Short notes describing the observed evidence or context behind the rating.
Capture useful context such as where the skill was observed, what standard was applied, or why the score was assigned.
Example: Demonstrated independent dashboard creation during monthly operations review.
📅Expiry_Date
InputDate PickerDate
Date after which the evidence should no longer be treated as current, if applicable.
Useful for certifications, time-bound validations, or assessments that should be refreshed after a specific period.
Example: 2028-03-09
📝Source_Document_Ref
DependentText InputText
Reference to the source file, report, or assessment artifact.
Use a document ID, LMS result reference, certificate number, or repository link for auditability.
Example: LMS-TEST-2026-0198
✅Final_Use_Flag
InputToggleBoolean
Indicates whether this evidence row is eligible to be considered during triangulation.
Allows data teams to exclude obsolete, duplicate, or low-quality evidence without deleting the original record.
Example: Yes
🔑Assessment_Record_IDRequired
PrimaryAuto GeneratedId
Unique identifier for each competency-assessment evidence record.
Use a stable record ID for each source-level competency evidence event so repeated evidence can be preserved separately.
Example: ECA-2026-000091
🔑Employee_IDRequired
DependentAuto GeneratedId
Unique identifier of the employee being assessed.
Links the competency evidence to the employee master and allows multiple evidence rows for the same employee-competency pair.
Example: EMP-01024
📝Employee_Name
DependentText InputText
Readable name of the assessed employee.
Included for review, reconciliation, and reporting; should match the employee master.
Example: Aarav Mehta
🔑Competency_IDRequired
PrimaryAuto GeneratedId
Unique identifier of the competency being assessed.
Use the official competency ID from the Competencies master to avoid duplicate or free-text competency records.
Example: COMP-0112
📝Competency_Name
InputText InputText
Readable name of the competency being assessed.
Business-facing competency label tied to the master competency taxonomy.
Example: Stakeholder Communication
📝Assessment_Source_Type
InputText InputText
Broad source category from which the competency evidence was obtained.
Examples include Self Assessment, Manager Assessment, Observation, Focus Group Discussion, Interview, Assessment Center, Peer Feedback, or Trainer Evaluation.
Example: Focus Group Discussion
📝Assessment_Source_Subtype
InputText InputText
More specific description of the evidence source.
Use when you need finer classification such as Panel Interview, Behavioral Event Interview, Center Simulation, or Structured FGD.
Example: Behavioral Event Interview
📝Assessment_Method
InputMulti SelectText
Method or instrument used to evaluate the competency.
Describes how the evidence was captured, such as behavioral rubric, 360 feedback, interview guide, simulation, observation checklist, or role-play.
Example: Behavioral rubric
📝Assessment_Cycle
InputText InputText
Assessment period or cycle in which the competency evidence was captured.
Use this field to group repeated competency assessments into formal cycles such as annual reviews, quarterly reviews, certification windows, or program-based evaluation rounds.
Example: FY2025-26 Annual Capability Review
📅Assessment_Date
InputDate PickerDate
Date on which the competency evidence was captured or validated.
Use the actual evidence date so repeated evaluations over time can be sequenced correctly.
Example: 2026-03-10
📆Assessment_Year
InputYear PickerYear
Calendar or fiscal year to which the assessment instance belongs.
Used for quick year-based filtering, reporting, and trend analysis without deriving the year repeatedly from Assessment_Date. This should align to the organization’s reporting convention, such as calendar year or fiscal year.
Example: 2026
📝Assessment_Quarter
InputText InputText
Quarter in which the assessment was conducted.
Captures the reporting quarter for the assessment instance so recurring assessments can be grouped into Q1, Q2, Q3, or Q4 for trend, cycle, and dashboard analysis.
Example: Q1
🔑Assessor_IDRequired
PrimaryAuto GeneratedId
Identifier of the person, panel, or system that produced the assessment.
Supports governance where competency assessments come from managers, assessors, panels, or systems.
Example: PANEL-004
📝Assessor_Name
InputText InputText
Readable name of the assessor, panel, or system owner.
Human-readable counterpart to Assessor_ID for review and traceability.
Example: Leadership Review Panel
📈Raw_Score
InputNumeric InputScore
Original score or rating captured from the source.
Store the direct result from the evidence source before normalization or consolidation.
Example: 3
📈Normalized_Score
InputNumeric InputScore
Standardized score converted to a common comparison scale.
Helps compare evidence coming from different scoring schemes such as 1-5, 1-7, or 0-100.
Example: 60
📈Score_Scale
InputNumeric InputScore
Scale used to interpret the source score.
Clarifies the scoring model used by the source so normalization remains auditable.
Example: 1-5
📝Proficiency_Level
InputDropdownText
Mapped competency proficiency level inferred from that single evidence source.
Use the standard level scale for source-level interpretation only; final validated level should be finalized after triangulation.
Example: Level 3 — Proficient
📈Confidence_Score
InputNumeric InputScore
Confidence or reliability score assigned to the source evidence.
Useful when some competency evidence sources should carry more or less influence in consolidation.
Example: 0.80
📝Validation_Status
InputDropdownText
Status showing whether the competency evidence is approved for downstream use.
Prevents draft, incomplete, or rejected evidence from being used in talent or gap analysis.
Example: Validated
📄Behavioral_Evidence_Notes
InputTextareaLongtext
Observed behavioral examples or qualitative context behind the rating.
Capture what the employee actually said or did that supports the competency score.
Example: Handled a cross-functional escalation calmly and aligned three teams on the response plan.
📅Expiry_Date
InputDate PickerDate
Date after which the evidence should no longer be treated as current, if applicable.
Useful where competency validations expire after a development cycle or reassessment period.
Example: 2027-03-31
📝Source_Document_Ref
DependentText InputText
Reference to the assessment file, panel note, report, or artifact.
Use a document ID, form ID, or repository reference for audit and review.
Example: PANEL-NOTE-2026-014
✅Final_Use_Flag
InputToggleBoolean
Indicates whether this evidence row is eligible for triangulation.
Allows teams to preserve history while excluding obsolete or low-trust evidence from final consolidation.
Example: Yes
🔑Employee_Certification_Record_IDRequired
DependentAuto GeneratedId
Unique identifier for each employee certification record.
Use a stable, non-reusable ID for each certification entry linked to an employee so certification history and renewals can be tracked clearly.
Example: ECERT-0001
🔑Employee_IDRequired
DependentAuto GeneratedId
Unique identifier of the employee holding the certification.
Links the certification to a specific employee record in Employee_Master for workforce capability and compliance analysis.
Example: EMP-0045
📝Employee_Name
DependentText InputText
Readable name of the employee holding the certification.
Included for review, reconciliation, and reporting; should match the employee master.
Example: Riya Sharma
📝Certification_Name
InputText InputText
Name of the certification held by the employee.
Capture the formal certification title exactly as issued by the certifying body.
Example: PMP
📝Certification_Category
InputDropdownText
Broad category of the certification.
Use a standard category such as Project Management, Cybersecurity, Quality, Cloud, or Data to support grouped analysis.
Example: Project Management
📝Certification_Domain
InputText InputText
Functional or skill domain to which the certification belongs.
Helps connect the certification to a broader workforce capability area or skill family.
Example: Leadership and Delivery
📝Issuing_Body
InputText InputText
Organization that issued the certification.
Record the certifying authority or awarding institution for auditability and external validation.
Example: PMI
📝Certification_Level
InputDropdownText
Level or tier of the certification, if applicable.
Use where certifications have graded or staged levels such as Foundation, Associate, Professional, or Expert.
Example: Professional
📅Issue_Date
InputDate PickerDate
Date on which the certification was issued.
Used to track certification history, current validity, and recency of credential acquisition.
Example: 2024-08-12
📅Expiry_Date
InputDate PickerDate
Date on which the certification expires, if applicable.
Helps identify renewal requirements and determine whether a certification is still valid for role or compliance purposes.
Example: 2027-08-11
📝Renewal_Status
InputDropdownText
Current renewal state of the certification.
Use controlled values such as Active, Renewed, Expired, Pending Renewal, or Not Applicable.
Example: Active
📝Certification_Status
InputDropdownText
Current status of the certification record.
Distinguishes active, expired, suspended, revoked, or in-progress certifications held by the employee.
Example: Active
🔑Certification_ID_or_License_NoRequired
DependentAuto GeneratedId
Official certificate number, license number, or external reference.
Useful for audit trail, external verification, and compliance checks where a credential number is required.
Example: PMI-8745632
✅Mandatory_for_Role_FlagRequired
InputToggleBoolean
Indicates whether the certification is mandatory for the employee’s current role.
Supports role-fit, compliance readiness, and mandatory capability tracking.
Example: Yes
🔑Linked_Role_IDRequired
DependentAuto GeneratedId
Role linked to the certification requirement or relevance.
Connect the certification to the most relevant role using the official role ID from Role_Architecture.
Example: ROLE-014
🔑Linked_Skill_IDRequired
PrimaryAuto GeneratedId
Skill most directly supported by the certification.
Use the primary skill that the certification validates or strengthens, based on the Skills master.
Example: SKL-021
🔑Linked_Competency_IDRequired
PrimaryAuto GeneratedId
Competency most directly informed by the certification, if applicable.
Use where the certification also supports broader applied capability or behavioral readiness.
Example: COMP-008
📈Certification_Score_or_Grade
InputNumeric InputScore
Score, grade, or result associated with the certification, if available.
Capture pass distinction, numerical score, or grade only when the issuing body provides a meaningful result.
Example: Pass with Distinction
✅Verified_Flag
InputToggleBoolean
Indicates whether the organization has verified the certification.
Supports governance and prevents unverified credentials from being treated as trusted evidence.
Example: Yes
📅Verification_Date
InputDate PickerDate
Date on which the certification was verified by the organization.
Tracks recency of credential validation and supports audit readiness.
Example: 2024-09-01
📝Verification_Source
InputText InputText
Source used to verify the certification.
Examples include issuer portal, uploaded document, HR validation, or third-party verification service.
Example: Issuer Portal
📝Training_Program_Linked
DependentText InputText
Associated internal training program related to this certification.
Use where the organization runs a linked preparation, readiness, or renewal program.
Example: Project Management Certification Prep Program
📝Business_Use_Relevance
InputText InputText
Short description of how the certification supports business needs.
Explain why the credential matters in practice, such as compliance, client delivery, project governance, or operational quality.
Example: Required for managing large cross-functional strategic projects
🔑Location_IDRequired
PrimaryAuto GeneratedId
Location associated with the employee holding the certification.
Supports location-level workforce capability views and compliance tracking.
Example: LOC-003
📄Remarks
InputTextareaLongtext
Additional notes or contextual comments.
Use for renewal reminders, pending documentation, exceptions, or other contextual observations.
Example: Renewal due next quarter
🔑Psychometric_Record_IDRequired
PrimaryAuto GeneratedId
Unique identifier for each psychometric assessment record.
Use a stable record ID for every psychometric result entry so it can be tracked across tools, dimensions, cycles, and retakes.
Example: PSY-000145
🔑Employee_IDRequired
DependentAuto GeneratedId
Unique identifier of the employee assessed.
Links the psychometric result to Employee_Master for development, role-fit, coaching, and talent discussions.
Example: EMP-01024
📝Employee_Name
DependentText InputText
Readable name of the assessed employee.
Human-readable employee reference for reporting and validation.
Example: Aarav Mehta
📅Assessment_Date
InputDate PickerDate
Date on which the psychometric assessment was completed.
Used for historical tracking, retakes, and validity control.
Example: 2026-03-10
📆Assessment_Year
InputYear PickerYear
Calendar or fiscal year to which the assessment instance belongs.
Used for quick year-based filtering, reporting, and trend analysis without deriving the year repeatedly from Assessment_Date. This should align to the organization’s reporting convention, such as calendar year or fiscal year.
Example: 2026
📝Assessment_Quarter
InputText InputText
Quarter in which the assessment was conducted.
Captures the reporting quarter for the assessment instance so recurring assessments can be grouped into Q1, Q2, Q3, or Q4 for trend, cycle, and dashboard analysis.
Example: Q1
📝Assessment_Cycle
InputText InputText
Assessment period or cycle in which the psychometric test was conducted.
Helps group records by leadership review, development program, annual talent cycle, or any structured assessment window used by the organization.
Example: FY2025-26 Leadership Review
📝Assessment_Tool_Name
InputText InputText
Name of the psychometric or behavioral assessment instrument used.
Identifies whether the result came from Big Five, DISC, MBTI, VIA, RISAC, or another approved tool.
Example: Big Five
📝Assessment_Tool_Category
InputDropdownText
Type/category of psychometric instrument.
Classifies the tool as personality, behavioral style, strengths, values, or vocational interest related.
Example: Personality Assessment
📝Assessment_Dimension
InputText InputText
Specific dimension, trait, or scale measured by the tool.
Each employee may have multiple rows for one tool because one row should represent one measured dimension.
Example: Conscientiousness
📝Dimension_Code
InputDropdownText
Short standardized code for the psychometric dimension.
Used for mapping, calculations, reporting, and downstream interpretation rules.
Example: BIG5-CON
📈Dimension_Score_Raw
InputNumeric InputScore
Raw score obtained in the psychometric dimension.
Stores the original result before normalization or index conversion.
Example: 34
📈Dimension_Score_Normalized
InputNumeric InputScore
Normalized or standardized score for comparison.
Converts the raw result to a common interpretive scale such as 0-100, decile, or sten.
Example: 78
📈Score_Scale
InputNumeric InputScore
Scale used to interpret the psychometric score.
Clarifies whether the tool output is reported as 0-100, 1-10, percentile, sten, or another scheme.
Example: 0-100
📝Result_Label
InputText InputText
Human-readable interpretation of the psychometric score.
Converts the score into an interpretable label for coaching or leadership-development use.
Example: High
📝Profile_Type
InputDropdownText
Composite profile type assigned by the instrument, if applicable.
Used where the tool produces an overall type or pattern label such as MBTI or DISC profile outputs.
Example: INTJ
📝Behavioral_Style
InputText InputText
Behavioral style or dominant style outcome from the assessment.
Useful for tools like DISC or similar behavior-style frameworks.
Example: Influence
📝Strength_Area
InputText InputText
Main strength area highlighted by the psychometric result.
Summarizes the strongest constructive pattern surfaced by the tool output.
Example: Analytical Thinking
📝Development_Area
InputText InputText
Main development area suggested by the psychometric result.
Captures a behavioral or developmental area that may need coaching or support.
Example: Stakeholder Influence
📝Assessment_Source
InputText InputText
Source through which the psychometric result was obtained.
Indicates whether the result came from an external provider, internal HR process, or digital platform.
Example: Certified External Provider
📝Administered_By
InputText InputText
Person, team, or vendor that administered the tool.
Useful for governance, follow-up, and auditability.
Example: Leadership Development Team
📝Validity_Status
InputDropdownText
Status indicating whether the psychometric result is valid for use.
Prevents outdated, incomplete, or unverified results from being used downstream.
Example: Valid
📝Confidence_Level
InputText InputText
Confidence or reliability level assigned to the result.
Indicates the degree to which the result is considered reliable for interpretation.
Example: High
📝Retake_Recommended
InputText InputText
Indicates whether reassessment is recommended.
Useful where the tool suggests reassessment after a development cycle, coaching period, or role change.
Example: No
📅Expiry_Date
InputDate PickerDate
Date after which the result should no longer be treated as current.
Some psychometric results may be considered stale after a specific period.
Example: 2028-03-09
📝Linked_Competency_Area
InputText InputText
Competency area that the psychometric result may inform contextually.
This is not the competency itself; it only shows which competency domain the result may help interpret.
Example: Collaboration
📝Linked_Leadership_Area
InputText InputText
Leadership area that the psychometric result may inform contextually.
Helps connect psychometric outcomes to leadership-development use cases without treating them as direct capability scores.
Example: Executive Presence
✅Use_for_Coaching_Flag
InputToggleBoolean
Indicates whether the result should be used in coaching or development planning.
Supports ethical use of psychometric data for development rather than blunt decision making.
Example: Yes
✅Use_for_Succession_Flag
DependentToggleBoolean
Indicates whether the result may be referenced in succession discussions.
Helps control where psychometric inputs can be used in talent reviews.
Example: Yes
✅Use_for_Role_Fit_Flag
InputToggleBoolean
Indicates whether the result may be used as contextual input for role-fit interpretation.
Should be used carefully and never as the sole decision factor.
Example: Yes
📄Assessment_Notes
InputTextareaLongtext
Additional interpretation notes or contextual remarks.
Capture observations, caveats, or important interpretation notes from HR, coach, or assessor.
Example: Strong analytical orientation; may need development in influence and relationship building.
📝Source_Document_Ref
DependentText InputText
Reference to the psychometric report, link, or document ID.
Useful for audit trail and detailed review of the underlying report.
Example: PSYREP-2026-0118

Data Triangulation

1 tabs · 31 fields
🔑Triangulation_Record_IDRequired
DependentAuto GeneratedId
Unique identifier for each consolidated triangulation record.
Use a stable ID for the final normalized capability view so it can be referenced in downstream gap analysis and audits.
Example: TRI-000501
🔑Employee_IDRequired
DependentAuto GeneratedId
Unique identifier of the employee whose evidence is being consolidated.
Links the final consolidated record to the employee master and the underlying evidence rows.
Example: EMP-01024
📝Employee_Name
InputText InputText
Readable name of the employee.
Human-readable employee label for review, reporting, and reconciliation.
Example: Aarav Mehta
📝Capability_Type
InputText InputText
Type of capability being triangulated.
Use Skill or Competency so one sheet can handle both consolidated capability views without duplicating design.
Example: Skill
🔑Capability_IDRequired
PrimaryAuto GeneratedId
Unique identifier of the skill or competency being triangulated.
Use the relevant master ID based on the capability type.
Example: SKL-0421
📝Capability_Name
InputText InputText
Readable name of the skill or competency being triangulated.
Business-facing label of the final capability object being consolidated.
Example: Data Analysis
📆Assessment_Year
InputYear PickerYear
Calendar or fiscal year associated with the consolidated assessment evidence.
Used for quick year-based reporting and downstream analysis after multiple source records are consolidated into a final triangulated capability view.
Example: 2026
📝Assessment_Quarter
InputText InputText
Quarter associated with the consolidated triangulated capability record.
Captures the reporting quarter tied to the evidence set or final triangulation decision so capability trends can be analyzed by quarter as well as year.
Example: Q1
📝Assessment_Cycle
InputText InputText
Assessment period or cycle represented by the triangulated capability record.
Use this field to identify the assessment window from which multiple evidence sources were consolidated, such as an annual capability review, quarterly review, or leadership assessment cycle.
Example: FY2025-26 Annual Capability Review
🔢Evidence_Source_Count
InputNumeric InputNumber
Number of accepted evidence records used in the triangulation.
Shows how many validated source rows contributed to the final consolidated result.
Example: 5
📝Source_Types_Used
InputText InputText
List or summary of source types used in the final consolidation.
Helps reviewers understand whether the final result is based on self, manager, observation, test, psychometric context, project evidence, or other inputs.
Example: Self Assessment; Manager Assessment; Observation; Test; Project Evidence
📈Self_Assessment_Score
InputNumeric InputScore
Standardized score contributed by the self-assessment source, if available.
Leave blank where self-assessment does not exist or is excluded from the final method.
Example: 60
📈Manager_Assessment_Score
InputNumeric InputScore
Standardized score contributed by the manager-assessment source, if available.
Used where manager validation forms part of the triangulation logic.
Example: 80
📈Observation_Score
InputNumeric InputScore
Standardized score contributed by observation-based evidence, if available.
Use for structured or unstructured observation evidence that is accepted into the final decision.
Example: 75
📈Focus_Group_Score
InputNumeric InputScore
Standardized score contributed by focus-group evidence, if available.
Useful where group-based discussions or panels are used to surface behavioral or capability evidence.
Example: 70
📈Test_Score
InputNumeric InputScore
Standardized score contributed by test-based evidence, if available.
Use for objective assessments, quizzes, or formal capability tests.
Example: 82
📈Certification_Evidence_Score
InputNumeric InputScore
Standardized score or equivalency contributed by certification evidence, if available.
Useful where a valid certification provides supporting proof of capability.
Example: 90
📈Project_Evidence_Score
InputNumeric InputScore
Standardized score contributed by project or work-sample evidence, if available.
Use where demonstrated work output is accepted as evidence of capability.
Example: 78
📈Psychometric_Context_Score
CalculatedNumeric InputScore
Contextual score derived from relevant psychometric evidence, if applicable.
Use carefully and only where policy allows psychometric signals to inform interpretation; do not use it as a direct substitute for skill evidence.
Example: 65
📈Weighted_Final_Score
InputNumeric InputScore
Final weighted or rule-based score after accepted evidence sources are consolidated.
This is the normalized consolidated score that downstream gap analysis should use rather than any single raw evidence row.
Example: 77
📝Final_Validated_Proficiency_Level
InputText InputText
Final proficiency level assigned after triangulation and validation.
Represents the official final evaluated level for this employee-capability combination within the cycle.
Example: Level 3 — Proficient
📝Required_Role_Proficiency_LevelRequired
Renamed from Required_Level
InputText InputText
Target or required level for the employee’s role on the same capability.
Pulled from role requirements so the final triangulated capability can be compared to expected proficiency.
Example: Level 4 — Advanced
📝Gap_vs_RequiredRequired
InputText InputText
Difference between the required level and the final validated level.
Can be stored as a numeric delta or labeled gap status; this creates a clean bridge into gap analysis sheets.
Example: 1
📈Confidence_Score
InputNumeric InputScore
Confidence score assigned to the final triangulated result.
