Reflects the overall trust in the final output based on source quality, recency, agreement, and validation.
Example: 0.86
📝Variance_Across_Sources
InputText InputText
Degree of variation across the accepted source scores.
Useful for identifying conflicting evidence or cases that may need manual review.
Example: 0.18
✅Outlier_Flag
InputToggleBoolean
Indicates whether one or more evidence sources materially deviated from the rest.
Used to trigger review where one source is inconsistent with the broader evidence pattern.
Example: No
📝Triangulation_Method
InputText InputText
Method used to produce the final consolidated result.
Document whether the final result came from weighted averaging, rule-based override, panel validation, or another approved method.
Example: Weighted Average with Manager Override
📝Validation_Status
InputDropdownText
Status indicating whether the triangulated result is final and approved for downstream use.
Prevents draft or unresolved consolidation outputs from feeding into gap analysis and intelligence outputs.
Example: Validated
📝Final_Reviewer
InputText InputText
Person, panel, or function that approved the final triangulated result.
Supports governance and auditability of the final capability decision.
Example: Capability Review Panel
📝Finalized_On
InputText InputText
Date on which the triangulated result was finalized.
Used to track the final approval and downstream freshness of the record.
Example: 2026-03-20
📝Source_Document_Ref
DependentText InputText
Reference to the triangulation pack, panel note, or consolidated evidence artifact.
Useful for audit trail and review of how the final result was determined.
Example: TRI-PACK-2026-008

Gap Analysis

3 tabs · 32 fields
🔑Employee_IDRequired
PrimaryAuto GeneratedId
Unique identifier of the employee for whom the skill gap is being analyzed.
Example: EMP-01024
📝Department
PrimaryText InputText
Department or function to which the employee belongs at the time of gap analysis.
Example: Data & Analytics
📝Role
InputText InputText
Current job role of the employee being assessed for skill gaps.
Example: Data Analyst
📝Skill
InputText InputText
Name of the skill being evaluated for gap measurement.
Example: SQL Querying
📝Required_Skill_Level
Renamed from Required_Level
InputText InputText
Target proficiency level required for the employee's role on this skill.
Example: Level 4 — Advanced
📝Current_Skill_Level
Renamed from Current_Level
InputText InputText
Validated current proficiency level of the employee on this skill.
Example: Level 3 — Proficient
📝Skill_Gap_Level
Renamed from Gap_Level
InputText InputText
Numeric or categorical gap between required and current skill proficiency.
Example: 1
📝Gap_Severity
InputText InputText
Severity classification of the skill gap based on business impact. Use Low, Medium, High, or Critical.
Example: Medium
📝Training_Required
InputText InputText
Indicates whether training or development intervention is needed to close the gap. Use Yes or No.
Example: Yes
📅Last_Updated
InputDate PickerDate
Date on which the skill gap record was last reviewed or refreshed.
Example: 2026-03-15
🔑Snapshot_IDRequired
PrimaryAuto GeneratedId
Unique identifier for the aggregated skill gap snapshot record.
Example: SGS-2026-042
📅Snapshot_Date
InputDate PickerDate
Date on which the skill gap snapshot was generated.
Example: 2026-03-15
📝Department
PrimaryText InputText
Department or function for which the snapshot is aggregated.
Example: Technology
📝Role
InputText InputText
Job role for which the snapshot summarizes skill gap data.
Example: Data Engineer
📝Skill
InputText InputText
Skill being summarized in the snapshot.
Example: Cloud Architecture
📝Required_Skill_Level
Renamed from Required_Level
InputText InputText
Target proficiency level required for the role on this skill.
Example: Level 4 — Advanced
📝Average_Current_Level
InputText InputText
Average assessed current proficiency level across employees in the snapshot scope.
Example: 2.8
📈Gap_Score
InputNumeric InputScore
Numeric gap score measuring the distance between required and average current level.
Example: 1.2
📝Employees_with_Gap
InputText InputText
Count of employees whose current proficiency is below the required level.
Example: 14
📝Gap_Severity
InputText InputText
Severity classification of the aggregated gap. Use Low, Medium, High, or Critical.
Example: High
📝Business_Risk_Level
InputText InputText
Level of business risk associated with the skill gap. Use Low, Medium, High, or Critical.
Example: High
📝Priority_Rank
InputText InputText
Priority ranking of this skill gap relative to other gaps for intervention planning.
Example: 3
🔑Employee_IDRequired
PrimaryAuto GeneratedId
Unique identifier of the employee for whom the competency gap is being analyzed.
Example: EMP-01024
📝Department
PrimaryText InputText
Department or function to which the employee belongs.
Example: Product Management
📝Role
InputText InputText
Current job role of the employee being assessed for competency gaps.
Example: Product Manager
📝Competency
InputText InputText
Name of the competency being evaluated for gap measurement.
Example: Stakeholder Management
📝Required_Competency_Level
Renamed from Required_Level
InputText InputText
Target competency proficiency level required for the role.
Example: Level 4 — Advanced
📝Current_Competency_Level
Renamed from Current_Level
InputText InputText
Validated current competency level of the employee.
Example: Level 3 — Proficient
📝Competency_Gap_Level
Renamed from Gap_Level
InputText InputText
Numeric or categorical gap between required and current competency level.
Example: 1
📝Gap_Impact
InputText InputText
Expected business or operational impact of the competency gap. Use Low, Medium, High, or Critical.
Example: Medium
📝Development_Action
InputText InputText
Recommended development action to close the competency gap.
Example: Enroll in stakeholder communication workshop
📅Last_Updated
InputDate PickerDate
Date on which the competency gap record was last reviewed.
Example: 2026-03-15

Capability Intelligence

4 tabs · 55 fields
🔑Index_IDRequired
PrimaryAuto GeneratedId
Unique identifier for the capability index definition.
Example: IDX-001
📈Index_Name
InputNumeric InputScore
Official name of the capability index.
Example: Workforce Capability Index
📝Abbreviation
InputText InputText
Short abbreviation or acronym used for the index in dashboards and reports.
Example: WCI
📝Index_Scope_Level
Renamed from Level
InputText InputText
Organizational level at which the index is calculated. Use Organization, Department, Role, or Employee.
Example: Organization
📝Business_Use
InputText InputText
Primary business purpose for which the index is used.
Example: Measure overall workforce capability readiness against strategic priorities
📝Calculation_Logic
InputText InputText
Formula or logic used to compute the index score.
Example: Weighted average of skill coverage, proficiency scores, and certification compliance
📝Primary_Data_Sources
InputText InputText
Main data sheets or systems providing input to the index calculation.
Example: Employee_Skill_Assessments, Data_Triangulation
📝Secondary_Data_Sources
InputText InputText
Supporting data sources that contribute to or validate the index.
Example: Training_Records, Employee_Certifications
📈Score_Range
InputNumeric InputScore
Minimum and maximum possible values for the index.
Example: 0–100
📝Benchmark_Floor
InputText InputText
Minimum acceptable benchmark score for the index.
Example: 60
📈Target_Score
InputNumeric InputScore
Target or aspirational score the organization aims to achieve.
Example: 80
📝Criticality_Floor
InputText InputText
Score below which the index triggers a critical alert.
Example: 45
📝Warning_Floor
InputText InputText
Score below which the index triggers a warning-level alert.
Example: 55
📝Review_Frequency
InputText InputText
How often the index definition and thresholds should be reviewed. Use Monthly, Quarterly, or Annual.
Example: Quarterly
📝Owner_Role
InputText InputText
Role or function accountable for maintaining and interpreting the index.
Example: Head of Capability Planning
📝Higher_Is_Better
InputText InputText
Indicates whether a higher index value represents a better outcome. Use Yes or No.
Example: Yes
📝Related_Indices
InputText InputText
Other capability indices that are related or complementary.
Example: Skill Readiness Index, Learning Culture Index
📄Notes
InputTextareaLongtext
Additional notes or interpretation guidance for the index.
Example: Exclude contractor population from calculation
🔑Index_IDRequired
PrimaryAuto GeneratedId
Unique identifier for the skills intelligence index record.
Example: SII-2026-Q1-014
📈Index_Name
InputNumeric InputScore
Name of the skills intelligence index being tracked.
Example: Skill Readiness Index
📈Index_Category
InputNumeric InputScore
Category grouping for the index. Use Readiness, Health, Risk, or Maturity.
Example: Readiness
📆Year
InputYear PickerYear
Calendar year for the index measurement.
Example: 2026
📝Quarter
InputText InputText
Quarter in which the index was calculated.
Example: Q1
📝Department
PrimaryText InputText
Department or function for which the index is computed.
Example: Technology
📝Role_Family
InputText InputText
Role family or job family used to segment the index.
Example: Data & Analytics
📈Index_Score
InputNumeric InputScore
Computed score for the index in the given period and scope.
Example: 72.5
📈Benchmark_Score
InputNumeric InputScore
Industry or internal benchmark score for comparison.
Example: 68.0
📝Gap_vs_Benchmark
InputText InputText
Difference between the index score and the benchmark score.
Example: +4.5
📝Trend_Direction
InputText InputText
Direction of change compared to the previous period. Use Improving, Stable, or Declining.
Example: Improving
📝Confidence_Level
InputText InputText
Confidence in the index score based on data quality and coverage. Use High, Medium, or Low.
Example: High
📝Calculation_Methodology
InputText InputText
Method used to compute the index score.
Example: Weighted average of triangulated skill levels vs role requirements
📝Data_Sources
InputText InputText
Data sheets or systems used as inputs for the calculation.
Example: Data_Triangulation, Role_Skill_Requirements
📄Notes
InputTextareaLongtext
Additional context or interpretation notes for the index value.
Example: Score reflects improved data coverage after Q4 assessment cycle
🔑CMM_IDRequired
PrimaryAuto GeneratedId
Unique identifier for the maturity model level definition.
Example: CMM-DIG-L3
📝Capability_Domain
InputText InputText
Capability domain to which the maturity model applies.
Example: Digital
📝Maturity_Level
InputText InputText
Numeric maturity level. Use 1 through 5.
Example: 3
📝Maturity_Level_Label
Renamed from Level_Label
InputText InputText
Readable label for the maturity level. Use Initial, Developing, Defined, Managed, or Optimized.
Example: Defined
📄Maturity_Level_Description
Renamed from Level_Description
InputTextareaLongtext
Narrative description of what the organization looks like at this maturity level.
Example: Standardized processes and skill frameworks are in place with consistent measurement across teams
📝Key_Characteristics
InputText InputText
Observable characteristics that indicate the organization is at this maturity level.
Example: Documented skill taxonomy, regular assessments, structured learning catalog
📝Evidence_Required
InputText InputText
Evidence or artifacts needed to validate maturity at this level.
Example: Assessment completion rates, taxonomy coverage, governance calendar adherence
📝Typical_Duration_To_Next_Level
InputText InputText
Estimated time in months to progress from this level to the next.
Example: 12–18 months
💰Investment_Band
InputNumeric InputCurrency
Typical investment range required to advance to the next maturity level.
Example: USD 50,000–150,000
📝Benchmark_Organisation_Type
InputText InputText
Type of organization typically found at this maturity level.
Example: Mid-size enterprise with 2–3 years of structured capability management
🔑Forecast_IDRequired
PrimaryAuto GeneratedId
Unique identifier for the maturity forecast record.
Example: CMF-2026-DIG
📆Year
InputYear PickerYear
Calendar year for the forecast.
Example: 2026
📝Capability_Domain
InputText InputText
Capability domain being forecasted.
Example: Digital
📝Current_Maturity_Level
InputText InputText
Current assessed maturity level for the domain. Use 1–5.
Example: 3
📝Current_Maturity_Label
Renamed from Current_Level_Label
InputText InputText
Readable label for the current capability maturity level. Use Initial, Developing, Defined, Managed, or Optimized.
Example: Defined
📝Projected_Maturity_Level
InputText InputText
Projected maturity level expected by end of the forecast period. Use 1–5.
Example: 4
📝Projected_Maturity_Label
Renamed from Projected_Level_Label
InputText InputText
Readable label for the projected future capability maturity level. Use Initial, Developing, Defined, Managed, or Optimized.
Example: Managed
📝Maturity_Gap_Level
Renamed from Gap_Level
InputText InputText
Gap between projected target and current maturity level.
Example: 1
📝Strategic_Risk
InputText InputText
Strategic risk if the maturity target is not achieved. Use Low, Medium, High, or Critical.
Example: Medium
💰Investment_Required
InputNumeric InputCurrency
Estimated investment required to achieve the projected maturity level in USD.
Example: 120000
📝Key_Enablers
InputText InputText
Key actions and enablers needed to achieve the maturity progression.
Example: Advanced analytics tools, structured coaching program, governance automation
📝Confidence_Level
InputText InputText
Confidence in the maturity forecast. Use High, Medium, or Low.
Example: Medium

Learning Needs

4 tabs · 58 fields
🔑LNA_IDRequired
PrimaryAuto GeneratedId
Unique identifier for the learning needs analysis record.
Example: LNA-2026-018
📆Year
InputYear PickerYear
Calendar year for which the learning need is identified.
Example: 2026
📝Department
PrimaryText InputText
Department or function where the learning need exists.
Example: Operations
📝Skill
InputText InputText
Skill for which the learning need has been identified.
Example: Process Automation
📝Skill_Gap_Level
InputText InputText
Severity of the skill gap driving the learning need. Use Low, Medium, High, or Critical.
Example: High
📝Employees_Affected
InputText InputText
Number of employees affected by this skill gap.
Example: 28
📝Business_Impact
InputText InputText
Expected business impact if the learning need is not addressed.
Example: Delayed automation rollout and increased manual processing cost
📝Business_Risk_Level
InputText InputText
Risk level to the business from the unaddressed learning need. Use Low, Medium, High, or Critical.
Example: High
📝Urgency_Level
InputText InputText
Time urgency for addressing the learning need. Use Immediate, Short-Term, Medium-Term, or Long-Term.
Example: Short-Term
📝Training_Required
InputText InputText
Indicates whether formal training intervention is needed. Use Yes or No.
Example: Yes
📝Recommended_Training
InputText InputText
Suggested training program or intervention to address the need.
Example: RPA and Process Automation Fundamentals
📝Non_Training_Intervention
InputText InputText
Non-training actions that can help close the gap, such as mentoring, rotation, or on-the-job learning.
Example: Pair with automation team for project shadowing
📝Priority_Level
InputText InputText
Overall priority assigned to the learning need for planning. Use Low, Medium, High, or Critical.
Example: High
🔑LNI_IDRequired
PrimaryAuto GeneratedId
Unique identifier for the individual learning needs identification record.
Example: LNI-2026-0412
📆Year
InputYear PickerYear
Calendar year for which the learning need is identified.
Example: 2026
🔑Employee_IDRequired
PrimaryAuto GeneratedId
Unique identifier of the employee with the identified learning need.
Example: EMP-01024
📝Department
PrimaryText InputText
Department to which the employee belongs.
Example: Analytics
📝Role
InputText InputText
Current role of the employee.
Example: Data Analyst
📝Skill_Gap
InputText InputText
Specific skill in which the gap has been identified.
Example: Advanced Statistical Modeling
📝Learning_Gap_Level
Renamed from Gap_Level
InputText InputText
Severity of the gap for this employee. Use Low, Medium, High, or Critical.
Example: Medium
📝Gap_Severity
InputText InputText
Business impact classification of the gap severity. Use Low, Medium, High, or Critical.
Example: Medium
📝Learning_Need_Type
InputText InputText
Type of learning need. Use Skill Gap, Compliance, Career Development, Role Readiness, or Reskilling.
Example: Skill Gap
📝Recommended_Intervention
InputText InputText
Suggested intervention to address the learning need.
Example: Enroll in Advanced Analytics certification prep
🔑Training_Program_IDRequired
PrimaryAuto GeneratedId
Identifier of the recommended training program from the catalog.
Example: TP-2026-022
📝Priority
InputText InputText
Priority level assigned to this learning need. Use Low, Medium, High, or Critical.
Example: High
📝Urgency
InputText InputText
Time urgency for addressing the need. Use Immediate, Short-Term, Medium-Term, or Long-Term.
Example: Short-Term
📝Manager_Approval_Status
InputText InputText
Current approval status of the learning intervention by the manager. Use Pending, Approved, or Rejected.
Example: Approved
🔑Roadmap_IDRequired
PrimaryAuto GeneratedId
Unique identifier for the capability roadmap entry.
Example: LCR-2026-Q2-008
📆Year
InputYear PickerYear
Calendar year for the roadmap planning period.
Example: 2026
📝Quarter
InputText InputText
Quarter in which the intervention is planned or expected.
Example: Q2
📝Department
PrimaryText InputText
Department or function targeted by the roadmap entry.
Example: Technology
📝Capability_Area
InputText InputText
Broad capability domain being addressed in the roadmap.
Example: Data & Analytics
📝Target_Skill
InputText InputText
Specific skill being targeted for improvement.
Example: Machine Learning
📝Current_Skill_Level
Renamed from Current_Level
InputText InputText
Average current proficiency level of employees on the target skill.
Example: 2.4
📝Target_Skill_Level
Renamed from Target_Level
InputText InputText
Desired proficiency level to achieve through the roadmap intervention.
Example: 3.5
📝Employees_Targeted
InputText InputText
Number of employees targeted for development under this roadmap entry.
Example: 18
📝Intervention_Type
InputText InputText
Type of learning or development intervention planned. Use Training, Certification, Mentoring, Rotation, or Blended.
Example: Blended
📝Training_Program
InputText InputText
Name or ID of the training program linked to the roadmap entry.
Example: ML Foundations for Analysts
💰Estimated_Investment
InputNumeric InputCurrency
Estimated cost of the planned intervention in USD.
Example: 32000
📝Expected_Impact
InputText InputText
Expected capability improvement or business outcome from the intervention.
Example: Move 60% of targeted employees to Level 3 proficiency
📝Priority
InputText InputText
Priority level of the roadmap entry. Use Low, Medium, High, or Critical.
Example: High
📝Roadmap_Status
Renamed from Status
InputText InputText
Current status of the roadmap entry. Use Planned, In Progress, Completed, or Deferred.
Example: In Progress
🔑Simulation_IDRequired
PrimaryAuto GeneratedId
Unique identifier for the investment simulation scenario.
Example: CIS-2026-014
📆Year
InputYear PickerYear
Calendar year for the simulation period.
Example: 2026
📝Department
PrimaryText InputText
Department or function being modeled in the simulation.
Example: Operations
📝Capability_Area
InputText InputText
Capability domain being modeled.
Example: Digital Operations
📝Skill
InputText InputText
Specific skill targeted in the simulation.
Example: Process Automation
📝Current_Skill_Level
Renamed from Current_Level
InputText InputText
Current average proficiency level for the targeted skill.
Example: 2.1
📝Target_Skill_Level
Renamed from Target_Level
InputText InputText
Target proficiency level the simulation aims to achieve.
Example: 3.5
📝Employees_Targeted
InputText InputText
Number of employees included in the simulation scenario.
Example: 35
💰Investment_USD
InputNumeric InputCurrency
Total investment amount modeled in the simulation in USD.
Example: 85000
📝Intervention_Type
InputText InputText
Type of capability intervention modeled. Use Training, Certification, Hiring, Mentoring, or Blended.
Example: Blended
📊Expected_Capability_Improvement_%
InputNumeric InputPercentage
Projected percentage improvement in capability level from the investment.
Example: 38%
📊Expected_Productivity_Impact_%
InputNumeric InputPercentage
Projected percentage improvement in productivity from the capability gain.
Example: 12%
📊Expected_Revenue_Impact_%
InputNumeric InputPercentage
Projected percentage impact on revenue attributable to the capability improvement.
Example: 4.5%
🔢Time_To_Impact_Months
InputNumeric InputNumber
Estimated number of months before the investment produces measurable results.
Example: 9
📝Scenario_Type
InputText InputText
Type of simulation scenario. Use Conservative, Moderate, Aggressive, or Custom.
Example: Moderate
📝Business_Risk_Level
InputText InputText
Business risk level if the capability investment is not made. Use Low, Medium, High, or Critical.
Example: High

Alerts & Thresholds

3 tabs · 24 fields
📝Ph10 · Alert_Threshold_Config — Criticality Floor Configuration for All SIP Metrics
InputText InputText
Master configuration sheet defining criticality floor thresholds for all SIP metrics used to trigger proactive alerts.
Example: Config record defining floor values for all capability indices
🔑Alert_IDRequired
PrimaryAuto GeneratedId
Unique identifier for the generated threshold alert.
Example: CTA-2026-00184
📅Alert_Date
InputDate PickerDate
Date on which the threshold breach alert was generated.
Example: 2026-03-15
🔑Config_IDRequired
PrimaryAuto GeneratedId
Identifier linking the alert to its threshold configuration rule.
Example: CFG-WCI-001
📝Metric_Category
InputText InputText
Category of the metric that triggered the alert. Use Capability, Workforce, Learning, Risk, or Compliance.
Example: Capability
📝Metric_Name
InputText InputText
Name of the specific metric that breached the threshold.
Example: Workforce Capability Index
📝Alert_Scope_Level
Renamed from Scope_Level
InputText InputText
Organizational level at which the breach occurred. Use Organization, Department, Role, or Location.
Example: Department
🔑Scope_IDRequired
PrimaryAuto GeneratedId
Identifier of the specific entity (department, role, location) where the breach occurred.
Example: DEPT-OPS
📝Scope_Name
InputText InputText
Readable name of the entity where the alert was triggered.
Example: Operations
📝Current_Value
InputText InputText
Actual current value of the metric at the time the alert was generated.
Example: 42.5
📝Threshold_Floor
InputText InputText
Configured floor value below which the alert triggers.
Example: 45.0
📝Gap_vs_Floor
InputText InputText
Numeric difference between the current value and the threshold floor.
Example: -2.5
📝Alert_Severity
InputText InputText
Severity of the alert based on how far the value is below the floor. Use Warning, Critical, or Emergency.
Example: Critical
📝Source_Sheet
InputText InputText
Name of the data sheet from which the metric value was sourced.
Example: Skills_Intelligence_Indices
📝Triggered_By
InputText InputText
System or process that detected and triggered the alert.
Example: Scheduled Index Refresh
📝Recommended_Action
InputText InputText
Suggested corrective or investigation action based on the alert.
Example: Review skill gap drivers in Operations and prioritize intervention
📝Alert_Status
Renamed from Status
InputText InputText
Current status of the alert. Use Open, Under Review, Resolved, or Dismissed.
Example: Open
🔑Alert_IDRequired
PrimaryAuto GeneratedId
Unique identifier for the proactive risk alert.
Example: PRA-2026-00042
📅Alert_Date
InputDate PickerDate
Date on which the proactive risk alert was generated.
Example: 2026-03-15
📝Risk_Type
InputText InputText
Type of workforce or capability risk detected. Use Attrition, Skill Gap, Succession, Compliance, or Capacity.
Example: Skill Gap
📝Department
PrimaryText InputText
Department or function where the risk has been detected.
Example: Engineering
📝Risk_Level
InputText InputText
Severity of the detected risk. Use Low, Medium, High, or Critical.
Example: High
📝Driver
InputText InputText
Primary factor or signal driving the risk alert.
Example: Cloud architecture skill coverage dropped below 50% in engineering team
📝Recommended_Action
InputText InputText
Suggested corrective or preventive action.
Example: Prioritize cloud architecture training and initiate targeted hiring

Predictive Analytics

5 tabs · 64 fields
🔑Forecast_IDRequired
PrimaryAuto GeneratedId
Unique identifier for the skill demand forecast record.
Example: SDF-2026-021
📆Year
InputYear PickerYear
Calendar year for the forecasted skill demand.
Example: 2027
📝Skill
InputText InputText
Name of the skill being forecasted.
Example: Generative AI
📝Skill_Domain
InputText InputText
Domain to which the forecasted skill belongs.
Example: Digital
📝Market_Demand_Level
InputText InputText
Projected market demand intensity for the skill. Use Low, Moderate, High, or Very High.
Example: Very High
📈Current_Demand_Index
InputNumeric InputScore
Current internal demand index score for the skill.
Example: 78
📊Projected_Demand_Growth_%
InputNumeric InputPercentage
Projected year-over-year growth in demand for the skill.
Example: 24%
📊Internal_Supply_%
InputNumeric InputPercentage
Percentage of internal workforce currently possessing the skill at required levels.
Example: 32%
📝Supply_Availability
InputText InputText
Availability of qualified talent in the external market. Use Scarce, Limited, Moderate, or Abundant.
Example: Scarce
📝Gap_Risk_Level
InputText InputText
Risk level of the projected supply-demand gap. Use Low, Medium, High, or Critical.
Example: Critical
📝Strategic_Relevance
InputText InputText
Strategic importance of the skill to the organization's future. Use Low, Medium, High, or Critical.
Example: Critical
📊Market_Salary_Premium_%
InputNumeric InputPercentage
Projected salary premium for the skill in the external market.
Example: 18%
📝Recommended_Action
InputText InputText
Suggested strategic action to address the forecasted skill demand.
Example: Launch internal reskilling program and initiate targeted external hiring
📝Data_Source
InputText InputText
Data sources used for the forecast projection.
Example: Market_Skill_Demand, labor analytics, and industry reports
🔑Prediction_IDRequired
PrimaryAuto GeneratedId
Unique identifier for the attrition risk prediction record.
Example: ARP-2026-0247
🔑Employee_IDRequired
PrimaryAuto GeneratedId
Unique identifier of the employee for whom attrition risk is predicted.
Example: EMP-0247
📆Year
InputYear PickerYear
Calendar year for the prediction.
Example: 2026
📝Department
PrimaryText InputText
Department to which the employee belongs.
Example: Technology
📝Role
InputText InputText
Current role of the employee.
Example: Senior Data Engineer
📝Job_Level
InputText InputText
Current job level or grade of the employee.
Example: L4
📆Tenure_Years
InputYear PickerYear
Employee tenure in years at the time of prediction.
Example: 3.2
📝Performance
InputText InputText
Most recent performance rating of the employee.
Example: 4.1
📈Attrition_Risk_Score
InputNumeric InputScore
Predicted probability or score of the employee leaving within the forecast period.
Example: 0.72
📝Risk_Category
InputText InputText
Categorized risk level based on the attrition score. Use Low, Medium, High, or Critical.
Example: High
📝Key_Drivers
InputText InputText
Primary factors contributing to the predicted attrition risk.
Example: Compensation gap, limited internal mobility, high external demand
📝Critical_Talent
InputText InputText
Indicates whether the employee is classified as critical talent. Use Yes or No.
Example: Yes
📝Recommended_Action
InputText InputText
Suggested retention or mitigation action for the employee.
Example: Initiate retention conversation and explore internal mobility options
📝Model
InputText InputText
Name or version of the predictive model used to generate the score.
Example: Attrition_Model_v3.2
📝Confidence
InputText InputText
Confidence level of the prediction. Use High, Medium, or Low.
Example: High
🔑Analysis_IDRequired
PrimaryAuto GeneratedId
Unique identifier for the statistical analysis record.
Example: STAT-2026-008
📝Analysis_Name
InputText InputText
Descriptive name of the statistical analysis performed.
Example: Training Hours vs Performance Rating Correlation
📝Analysis_Type
InputText InputText
Type of statistical method used. Use Correlation, Regression, Clustering, Comparison, or Predictive.
Example: Correlation
📝Independent_Variable
InputText InputText
Independent variable or predictor used in the analysis.
Example: Training_Hours_Completed
📝Dependent_Variable
InputText InputText
Dependent variable or outcome being measured.
Example: Performance_Rating
📝Correlation_Coefficient
InputText InputText
Correlation coefficient value from the analysis.
Example: 0.64
📝R_Squared
InputText InputText
R-squared value indicating the proportion of variance explained.
Example: 0.41
📝P_Value
InputText InputText
Statistical significance p-value of the analysis result.
Example: 0.002
📝Confidence_Interval
InputText InputText
Confidence interval for the statistical estimate.
Example: 95% CI: 0.48–0.76
📝Sample_Size
InputText InputText
Number of observations or employees included in the analysis.
Example: 842
📆Year
InputYear PickerYear
Calendar year for which the analysis was performed.
Example: 2026
📝Scope
InputText InputText
Organizational scope of the analysis. Use Organization, Department, Role Family, or Location.
Example: Organization
📝Business_Implication
InputText InputText
Business interpretation or implication of the statistical finding.
Example: Employees with more training hours show meaningfully higher performance ratings
📝Recommended_Action
InputText InputText
Suggested action based on the statistical insight.
Example: Increase targeted training allocation for mid-level performers
🔑Opportunity_IDRequired
PrimaryAuto GeneratedId
Unique identifier for the strategic capability opportunity.
Example: SCO-2026-011
📝Capability
InputText InputText
Capability area where the strategic opportunity has been identified.
Example: AI and Machine Learning
📝Business_Area
InputText InputText
Business function or unit that would benefit from the capability investment.
Example: Product Development
📝Opportunity_Type
InputText InputText
Type of capability opportunity. Use Build, Buy, Partner, Reskill, or Automate.
Example: Build
📝Impact_Level
InputText InputText
Expected impact level of pursuing the opportunity. Use Low, Medium, High, or Transformational.
Example: Transformational
💰Investment_Required
InputNumeric InputCurrency
Estimated investment required to pursue the opportunity in USD.
Example: 250000
📝Time_To_Value
InputText InputText
Estimated time to realize value from the investment.
Example: 12–18 months
📝Strategic_Rationale
InputText InputText
Business rationale for pursuing the capability opportunity.
Example: AI-enabled product features are the primary growth lever for next fiscal year
📝Intervention_Approach
InputText InputText
Planned approach to build or acquire the capability.
Example: Internal reskilling program combined with two senior AI hires
📝Expected_Outcome
InputText InputText
Expected business or capability outcome from the intervention.
Example: Establish in-house AI capability serving 3 product lines within 18 months
🔑Model_IDRequired
PrimaryAuto GeneratedId
Unique identifier for the predictive model.
Example: PMR-ATT-v3.2
📝Model_Name
InputText InputText
Descriptive name of the predictive model.
Example: Employee Attrition Risk Predictor
📝Prediction_Target
InputText InputText
Variable or outcome the model is designed to predict.
Example: Probability of voluntary exit within 12 months
📝Input_Features
InputText InputText
Key input variables or features used by the model.
Example: Tenure, performance, compensation gap, manager rating, skill gap, engagement signals
📝Algorithm_Type
InputText InputText
Type of algorithm or method used. Use Logistic Regression, Random Forest, Gradient Boosting, Neural Network, or Ensemble.
Example: Gradient Boosting
📝Training_Data_Sources
InputText InputText
Data sources used to train the model.
Example: Employee_Master, Employee_Yearly_Performance, Employee_Exit, Training_Records
📈Accuracy_Score
InputNumeric InputScore
Model accuracy or performance metric value.
Example: 0.84 AUC
📅Last_Trained_Date
InputDate PickerDate
Date on which the model was last trained or retrained.
Example: 2026-02-28
📝Refresh_Frequency
InputText InputText
How often the model is retrained. Use Monthly, Quarterly, Semi-Annual, or Annual.
Example: Quarterly
📝Model_Status
Renamed from Status
InputText InputText
Current operational status of the model. Use Active, Under Review, Retired, or In Development.
Example: Active
📄Confidence_Notes
InputTextareaLongtext
Notes on model reliability, limitations, or known biases.
Example: Performance drops for employees with less than 6 months tenure due to limited data

Journey Tracking

7 tabs · 88 fields
🔑Journey_IDRequired
PrimaryAuto GeneratedId
Unique identifier for the organization journey record.
Example: OJ-2026
📆Year
InputYear PickerYear
Calendar year for the journey snapshot.
Example: 2026
💰Revenue_USD_Million
InputNumeric InputCurrency
Total annual revenue in USD millions.
Example: 312.5
📊Revenue_Growth_%
InputNumeric InputPercentage
Year-over-year revenue growth percentage.
Example: 9.1%
📝Workforce_Size
InputText InputText
Total employee headcount for the year.
Example: 1320
📝New_Locations
InputText InputText
Number of new locations opened during the year.
Example: 3
📈Org_Skill_Health_Index
InputNumeric InputScore
Organization-wide skill health index score.
Example: 72.4
📈Workforce_Capability_Index
InputNumeric InputScore
Composite workforce capability index score.
Example: 68.9
📈Skill_Readiness_Index
InputNumeric InputScore
Skill readiness index measuring preparedness for current roles.
Example: 74.2
📈Learning_Culture_Index
InputNumeric InputScore
Score reflecting the strength of the learning culture.
Example: 78.5
💰Training_Investment_USD
InputNumeric InputCurrency
Total annual investment in training and development in USD.
Example: 485000
🔢Training_Hours_Per_Employee
InputNumeric InputNumber
Average training hours completed per employee.
Example: 22.4
📊Attrition_Rate_%
InputNumeric InputPercentage
Overall annual attrition rate as a percentage.
Example: 12.1%
📊Internal_Promotion_Rate_%
InputNumeric InputPercentage
Percentage of roles filled through internal promotion.
Example: 7.2%
📝Capability_Maturity_Level
InputText InputText
Current capability maturity level of the organization. Use 1–5.
Example: 3
📝Key_Capability_Developments
InputText InputText
Summary of major capability milestones achieved during the year.
Example: Launched enterprise skill taxonomy and completed first full assessment cycle
🔑Journey_IDRequired
PrimaryAuto GeneratedId
Unique identifier for the department journey record.
Example: DJ-2026-TECH
📆Year
InputYear PickerYear
Calendar year for the journey snapshot.
Example: 2026
📝Department
PrimaryText InputText
Department being tracked.
Example: Technology
📈Org_Skill_Health_Index
InputNumeric InputScore
Department-level skill health index score.
Example: 74.8
📈Workforce_Capability_Index
InputNumeric InputScore
Department-level workforce capability index score.
Example: 71.2
📈Skill_Readiness_Index
InputNumeric InputScore
Department-level skill readiness score.
Example: 76.1
🔢Avg_Training_Hours
InputNumeric InputNumber
Average training hours per employee in the department.
Example: 26.3
📝Key_Skill_Gaps
InputText InputText
Most significant skill gaps in the department.
Example: Cloud Architecture, AI/ML, Advanced Data Engineering
📝Improvement_Actions
InputText InputText
Key capability improvement actions taken during the year.
Example: Launched cloud certification program and AI readiness bootcamp
🔑Journey_IDRequired
PrimaryAuto GeneratedId
Unique identifier for the workforce journey record.
Example: WJ-2026
📆Year
InputYear PickerYear
Calendar year for the journey snapshot.
Example: 2026
🔢Total_Employees
InputNumeric InputNumber
Total active employee count.
Example: 1320
📝New_Hires
InputText InputText
Number of employees hired during the year.
Example: 185
🔢Attrition_Count
InputNumeric InputNumber
Number of employees who exited during the year.
Example: 142
📊Attrition_Rate_%
InputNumeric InputPercentage
Annual attrition rate as a percentage.
Example: 10.8%
📝Internal_Promotions
InputText InputText
Number of employees promoted internally during the year.
Example: 78
🔢Avg_Training_Hours_Per_Person
InputNumeric InputNumber
Average training hours completed per employee.
Example: 22.4
📈Learning_Culture_Index
InputNumeric InputScore
Score reflecting workforce-wide learning culture strength.
Example: 78.5
📈Talent_Retention_Index
InputNumeric InputScore
Index measuring effectiveness of talent retention efforts.
Example: 81.2
📝Leadership_Pipeline_Strength
InputText InputText
Assessment of leadership pipeline depth. Use Strong, Moderate, Weak, or Critical.
Example: Moderate
📝Succession_Readiness
InputText InputText
Overall succession planning readiness. Use High, Medium, or Low.
Example: Medium
📝Critical_Role_Risk
InputText InputText
Risk level for unfilled or at-risk critical roles. Use Low, Medium, High, or Critical.
Example: Medium
🔑Journey_IDRequired
PrimaryAuto GeneratedId
Unique identifier for the employee journey record.
Example: EJ-2026-01024
🔑Employee_IDRequired
PrimaryAuto GeneratedId
Unique identifier of the employee.
Example: EMP-01024
📆Year
InputYear PickerYear
Calendar year for the journey snapshot.
Example: 2026
📝Department
PrimaryText InputText
Department of the employee during the year.
Example: Analytics
📝Role
InputText InputText
Role held by the employee during the year.
Example: Data Analyst
📈Performance_Rating
InputNumeric InputScore
Performance rating received for the year.
Example: 4.2
📝Performance_Label
InputText InputText
Descriptive performance label for the year.
Example: Exceeds Expectations
📝Skill_Proficiency_Avg
InputText InputText
Average skill proficiency score across assessed skills.
Example: 3.6
✅Active_Skill_Gaps
InputToggleBoolean
Number of active skill gaps the employee has.
Example: 2
📝Training_Programs_Completed
InputText InputText
Number of training programs completed during the year.
Example: 4
🔢Training_Hours
InputNumeric InputNumber
Total training hours completed during the year.
Example: 48
📝Certifications_Earned
InputText InputText
Number of new certifications earned during the year.
Example: 1
📝Career_Progression_Event
InputText InputText
Career event that occurred during the year, if any. Use Promotion, Lateral Move, Role Change, or None.
Example: None
📝Manager_Recommendation
InputText InputText
Manager recommendation from the year-end review.
Example: Prepare for senior analyst role within 12 months
🔑Journey_IDRequired
PrimaryAuto GeneratedId
Unique identifier for the role journey record.
Example: RJ-2026-DA
📆Year
InputYear PickerYear
Calendar year for the role journey snapshot.
Example: 2026
📝Role_Title
PrimaryText InputText
Standard title of the role being tracked.
Example: Data Analyst
📝Required_Skills
InputText InputText
List of skills required for the role in the given year.
Example: SQL, Python, Data Visualization, Statistical Analysis
📝Critical_Skills
InputText InputText
Skills classified as critical for role performance.
Example: SQL, Statistical Analysis
🔢Skill_Count
InputNumeric InputNumber
Total number of skills mapped to the role.
Example: 8
📝Technology_Changes
InputText InputText
Technology or tool changes affecting the role during the year.
Example: Migration to cloud-based analytics platform
📝Capability_Expectation_Level
InputText InputText
Expected overall capability level for the role. Use Foundational, Intermediate, Advanced, or Expert.
Example: Intermediate
📝Future_Skill_Requirements
InputText InputText
Emerging skill requirements anticipated for the role.
Example: Machine Learning basics, GenAI prompt engineering
📝Maturity_Expectation
InputText InputText
Expected maturity of role capability in the organization. Use Nascent, Developing, Established, or Mature.
Example: Established
🔑Journey_IDRequired
PrimaryAuto GeneratedId
Unique identifier for the learning journey record.
Example: LJ-2026-01024-014
🔑Employee_IDRequired
PrimaryAuto GeneratedId
Unique identifier of the employee.
Example: EMP-01024
📆Year
InputYear PickerYear
Calendar year of the learning event.
Example: 2026
📝Department
PrimaryText InputText
Department of the employee during the learning event.
Example: Analytics
📝Role
InputText InputText
Role held during the learning event.
Example: Data Analyst
📝Training_Program
InputText InputText
Name of the training program completed.
Example: Advanced Data Analysis for Managers
📝Program_Category
InputText InputText
Category of the training program.
Example: Technical
📝Skill_Improved
InputText InputText
Skill that was improved through the training program.
Example: Data Analysis
📝Skill_Level_Before
InputText InputText
Skill proficiency level before the training. Use Level 1–5 scale.
Example: Level 2 — Developing
📝Skill_Level_After
InputText InputText
Skill proficiency level after the training. Use Level 1–5 scale.
Example: Level 3 — Proficient
📝Skill_Level_Improvement
Renamed from Level_Improvement
InputText InputText
Numeric improvement in skill level achieved through training.
Example: 1
📈Assessment_Score
InputNumeric InputScore
Post-training assessment score achieved.
Example: 86
📝Certification_Achieved
InputText InputText
Indicates whether a certification was achieved. Use Yes or No.
Example: No
📝Delivery_Mode
InputText InputText
Delivery mode of the training. Use Classroom, Virtual, E-Learning, Blended, or On-the-Job.
Example: Virtual Instructor-Led
🔑Journey_IDRequired
PrimaryAuto GeneratedId
Unique identifier for the skill demand journey record.
Example: SDJ-2026-ML
📆Year
InputYear PickerYear
Calendar year for the demand snapshot.
Example: 2026
📝Skill_Name
PrimaryText InputText
Name of the skill being tracked.
Example: Machine Learning
📝Skill_Domain
InputText InputText
Domain to which the skill belongs.
Example: Digital
📈Demand_Index
InputNumeric InputScore
Numeric index representing overall demand for the skill.
Example: 82
📝Demand_Level
InputText InputText
Categorized demand level. Use Low, Moderate, High, or Very High.
Example: High
📈Supply_Index
InputNumeric InputScore
Numeric index representing internal supply of the skill.
Example: 48
📝Supply_Level
InputText InputText
Categorized supply level. Use Scarce, Limited, Moderate, or Sufficient.
Example: Limited
📈Gap_Score
InputNumeric InputScore
Numeric gap between demand and supply indices.
Example: 34
📝Skill_Gap_Level
Renamed from Gap_Level
InputText InputText
Categorized gap level. Use Low, Medium, High, or Critical.
Example: High
📝Strategic_Importance
InputText InputText
Strategic importance of the skill. Use Low, Medium, High, or Critical.
Example: Critical
📝Future_Demand_Forecast
InputText InputText
Summary of projected future demand trajectory.
Example: Demand expected to grow 20%+ year-over-year driven by AI product roadmap

Skill Graph

2 tabs · 18 fields
🔑Node_IDRequired
PrimaryAuto GeneratedId
Unique identifier for the node in the skill graph.
Example: NODE-SKL-0421
📝Node_Type
InputText InputText
Type of entity represented by the node. Use Skill, Competency, Role, Department, Domain, or Employee.
Example: Skill
📝Node_Name
InputText InputText
Display name of the graph node.
Example: Data Analysis
📝Domain
InputText InputText
Domain or category the node belongs to.
Example: Digital
📝Sub_Type
InputText InputText
More specific classification of the node within its type.
Example: Technical Skill
📝Attribute_1
InputText InputText
First custom attribute associated with the node for enrichment.
Example: Emerging: Yes
📝Attribute_2
InputText InputText
Second custom attribute associated with the node.
Example: Criticality: High
📝Source_Phase
InputText InputText
Data processing phase from which the node was generated. Use Input, Processing, or Output.
Example: Input
📝Source_Sheet
InputText InputText
Name of the source data sheet from which the node was derived.
Example: Skills
🔑Edge_IDRequired
PrimaryAuto GeneratedId
Unique identifier for the edge connecting two nodes in the skill graph.
Example: EDGE-0421-0422
🔑Source_Node_IDRequired
PrimaryAuto GeneratedId
Identifier of the source node in the edge relationship.
Example: NODE-SKL-0421
🔑Target_Node_IDRequired
PrimaryAuto GeneratedId
Identifier of the target node in the edge relationship.
Example: NODE-SKL-0422
📝Relationship_Type
InputText InputText
Type of relationship between nodes. Use Requires, Enables, Related_To, Prerequisite, Complements, or Part_Of.
Example: Requires
📝Weight_or_Strength
InputText InputText
Numeric weight or strength of the relationship. Use 0.0 to 1.0.
Example: 0.85
📆Year
InputYear PickerYear
Calendar year associated with the edge relationship.
Example: 2026
📝Source_Node_Name
InputText InputText
Readable name of the source node.
Example: Data Analysis
📝Target_Node_Name
InputText InputText
Readable name of the target node.
Example: SQL Querying
📄Notes
InputTextareaLongtext
Additional context about the relationship between nodes.
Example: SQL is a foundational prerequisite for data analysis work

AI Recommendations

4 tabs · 77 fields
🔑Recommendation_IDRequired
PrimaryAuto GeneratedId
Unique identifier for the AI-generated skill recommendation.
Example: ASR-2026-00412
🔑Employee_IDRequired
PrimaryAuto GeneratedId
Unique identifier of the employee receiving the recommendation.
Example: EMP-01024
📝Department
PrimaryText InputText
Department of the employee.
Example: Analytics
📝Current_Role
InputText InputText
Current role of the employee.
Example: Data Analyst
📝Recommended_Skill
InputText InputText
Skill recommended for development.
Example: Machine Learning
📝Recommendation_Type
InputText InputText
Type of recommendation. Use Upskill, Reskill, Cross-Skill, Compliance, or Future-Ready.
Example: Upskill
📝Recommendation_Source
InputText InputText
Source or model that generated the recommendation.
Example: Skill_Gap_Engine_v2.1
📝Reason_For_Recommendation
InputText InputText
Explanation of why this skill was recommended.
Example: Role evolution requires ML capability; current gap is 2 levels
📝Skill_Gap_Level
InputText InputText
Current gap level for the recommended skill. Use Low, Medium, High, or Critical.
Example: High
📝Current_Skill_Level
Renamed from Current_Level
InputText InputText
Employee's current proficiency level on the recommended skill. Use Level 1–5.
Example: Level 1 — Awareness
📝Target_Skill_Level
Renamed from Target_Level
InputText InputText
Target proficiency level to be achieved. Use Level 1–5.
Example: Level 3 — Proficient
📝Priority
InputText InputText
Priority of the recommendation. Use Low, Medium, High, or Critical.
Example: High
📝Regulatory_Skill
InputText InputText
Indicates whether the recommended skill has regulatory or compliance relevance. Use Yes or No.
Example: No
📝Suggested_Training_Program
InputText InputText
Name of the suggested training program for the skill.
Example: ML Foundations for Analysts
🔑Training_Program_IDRequired
PrimaryAuto GeneratedId
Identifier of the suggested training program.
Example: TP-2026-022
📝Business_Impact
InputText InputText
Expected business impact of acting on the recommendation.
Example: Enable predictive analytics capability in the team
📈Performance_Rating_2024
InputNumeric InputScore
Employee's most recent performance rating used as input.
Example: 4.2
📈Confidence_Score
InputNumeric InputScore
Confidence score of the recommendation. Use 0.0 to 1.0.
Example: 0.88
📅Generated_Date
InputDate PickerDate
Date on which the recommendation was generated.
Example: 2026-03-15
📝Model_Used
InputText InputText
Name or version of the AI model that generated the recommendation.
Example: SkillRec_Engine_v2.1
🔑Recommendation_IDRequired
PrimaryAuto GeneratedId
Unique identifier for the AI-generated training recommendation.
Example: ATR-2026-00318
🔑Employee_IDRequired
PrimaryAuto GeneratedId
Unique identifier of the employee.
Example: EMP-01024
📝Department
PrimaryText InputText
Department of the employee.
Example: Analytics
📝Role
InputText InputText
Current role of the employee.
Example: Data Analyst
📝Recommended_Training
InputText InputText
Name of the recommended training program.
Example: Advanced Statistical Modeling with Python
🔑Training_Program_IDRequired
PrimaryAuto GeneratedId
Identifier of the recommended training program.
Example: TP-2026-031
📝Target_Skill
InputText InputText
Skill the training program is designed to improve.
Example: Statistical Modeling
📝Skill_Gap_Level
InputText InputText
Gap level the training is intended to address. Use Low, Medium, High, or Critical.
Example: Medium
📝Learning_Pathway
InputText InputText
Suggested learning pathway. Use Foundation, Intermediate, Advanced, Specialist, or Leadership.
Example: Advanced
📝Delivery_Mode
InputText InputText
Recommended delivery mode. Use Classroom, Virtual, E-Learning, Blended, or On-the-Job.
Example: Virtual Instructor-Led
🔢Recommended_Duration_Hours
InputNumeric InputNumber
Suggested total duration for the training in hours.
Example: 24
🔢Current_Annual_Training_Hours
InputNumeric InputNumber
Employee's training hours completed in the current year so far.
Example: 32
📝Expected_Impact
InputText InputText
Expected capability improvement from completing the training.
Example: Move from Level 2 to Level 3 in statistical modeling
📝Urgency
InputText InputText
Time urgency for the training. Use Immediate, Short-Term, Medium-Term, or Long-Term.
Example: Short-Term
📝Regulatory_Training
InputText InputText
Indicates whether the training is required for compliance. Use Yes or No.
Example: No
💰Estimated_Cost_USD
InputNumeric InputCurrency
Estimated cost of the training per participant in USD.
Example: 650
📝Expected_ROI_Indicator
InputText InputText
Qualitative ROI expectation. Use Low, Medium, High, or Very High.
Example: High
📈Confidence_Score
InputNumeric InputScore
Confidence score of the recommendation. Use 0.0 to 1.0.
Example: 0.82
📅Generated_Date
InputDate PickerDate
Date on which the recommendation was generated.
Example: 2026-03-15
📝Model_Used
InputText InputText
AI model used to generate the recommendation.
Example: TrainingRec_Engine_v1.8
🔑Prediction_IDRequired
PrimaryAuto GeneratedId
Unique identifier for the career path prediction.
Example: ACP-2026-01024
🔑Employee_IDRequired
PrimaryAuto GeneratedId
Unique identifier of the employee.
Example: EMP-01024
📝Department
PrimaryText InputText
Current department of the employee.
Example: Analytics
📝Current_Role
InputText InputText
Current role of the employee.
Example: Data Analyst
📝Current_Job_Level
InputText InputText
Current job level of the employee.
Example: L3
📝Potential_Future_Role
InputText InputText
Predicted future role the employee is most likely to move into.
Example: Senior Data Analyst
📝Future_Job_Level
InputText InputText
Predicted job level of the future role.
Example: L4
📝Required_Skills_For_Role
InputText InputText
Skills required for the predicted future role.
Example: Advanced SQL, Machine Learning, Stakeholder Communication, Data Strategy
📝Skills_Already_Demonstrated
InputText InputText
Skills the employee already has at the required level.
Example: Advanced SQL, Data Visualization
📝Skill_Gap_To_Bridge
InputText InputText
Skills the employee needs to develop to be ready for the predicted role.
Example: Machine Learning, Stakeholder Communication
📝Recommended_Development_Path
InputText InputText
Suggested development path to bridge the gaps.
Example: ML certification + stakeholder management workshop + cross-functional project
📝Readiness_Level
InputText InputText
Employee's current readiness for the predicted role. Use Ready Now, Ready in 1–2 Years, or Ready in 3–5 Years.
Example: Ready in 1–2 Years
📝Estimated_Timeline
InputText InputText
Estimated time to reach readiness for the predicted role.
Example: 14 months
📝Manager_Signal
InputText InputText
Manager's assessment or signal regarding the employee's career trajectory.
Example: Supportive — recommended for next-level preparation
📈Performance_Rating_2024
InputNumeric InputScore
Most recent performance rating used as model input.
Example: 4.2
📝Data_Sources_Used
InputText InputText
Data sources used by the prediction model.
Example: Performance, skills, mobility, succession, training records
📈Confidence_Score
InputNumeric InputScore
Confidence score of the prediction. Use 0.0 to 1.0.
Example: 0.79
📅Prediction_Date
InputDate PickerDate
Date on which the prediction was generated.
Example: 2026-03-15
📝Model_Used
InputText InputText
AI model used to generate the career path prediction.
Example: CareerPath_Predictor_v2.0
🔑Forecast_IDRequired
PrimaryAuto GeneratedId
Unique identifier for the future skill forecast record.
Example: AFSF-2027-GenAI
📆Forecast_Year
InputYear PickerYear
Target year for the skill demand forecast.
Example: 2027
📝Skill_Name
PrimaryText InputText
Name of the skill being forecasted.
Example: Generative AI
📝Skill_Domain
InputText InputText
Domain to which the skill belongs.
Example: Digital
📝Current_Demand_Level_2024
InputText InputText
Baseline demand level for the skill as of the reference year. Use Low, Moderate, High, or Very High.
Example: High
📝Projected_Demand_Level
InputText InputText
Projected demand level in the forecast year. Use Low, Moderate, High, or Very High.
Example: Very High
📊Demand_Growth_%
InputNumeric InputPercentage
Projected growth in demand from baseline to forecast year.
Example: 35%
✅Emerging_Skill_Flag
InputToggleBoolean
Indicates whether the skill is classified as emerging. Use Yes or No.
Example: Yes
📝Internal_Readiness_2024
InputText InputText
Organization's current readiness level for the skill. Use Low, Medium, or High.
Example: Low
📝Strategic_Priority
InputText InputText
Strategic importance of the skill. Use Low, Medium, High, or Critical.
Example: Critical
📝Time_Horizon
InputText InputText
Planning time horizon. Use Short-Term (1yr), Medium-Term (2–3yr), or Long-Term (4–5yr).
Example: Short-Term
📝Industry_Driver
InputText InputText
Industry or technology trend driving demand for the skill.
Example: Rapid enterprise adoption of generative AI across business functions
💰Recommended_Capability_Investment
InputNumeric InputCurrency
Suggested investment action to address the forecasted demand.
Example: Launch internal GenAI academy and sponsor external certifications
💰Investment_Urgency
InputNumeric InputCurrency
Urgency of the recommended investment. Use Immediate, Short-Term, Medium-Term, or Long-Term.
Example: Immediate
📝Forecast_Confidence_Level
InputText InputText
Confidence in the forecast accuracy. Use High, Medium, or Low.
Example: Medium
📝Data_Sources
InputText InputText
Data sources used for the forecast.
Example: Market_Skill_Demand, industry reports, internal skill assessments
📝Model_Used
InputText InputText
AI model used to generate the forecast.
Example: FutureSkill_Forecaster_v1.5
📅Generated_Date
InputDate PickerDate
Date on which the forecast was generated.
Example: 2026-03-15
